column3: question
column4: choices
column6: explanation

column3: Universal containers (UC) has an e-commerce website while customers can buy products, make payments, and manage their accounts. UC decides to build a customer Community on Salesforce and wants to allow the customers to access the community for their accounts without logging in again. UC decides to implement an sp-Initiated SSO using a SAML-BASED compliant IDP. In this scenario where salesforce is the service provider, which two activities must be performed in salesforce to make sp-Initiated SSO work? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Configure SAML SSO settings. | B. Configure Delegated Authentication | C. Create a connected App | D. Set up my domain
column6: SP-initiated SSO with Salesforce as the Service Provider requires: (A) Configure SAML SSO settings in Salesforce to define the IdP certificate, issuer, and ACS URL; (D) Set up My Domain, which is a prerequisite for SP-initiated SSO as it provides the unique Salesforce URL that triggers the IdP redirect. Delegated Authentication (B) is a different mechanism, and a Connected App (C) is not required for SAML SSO.

column3: The security team at Universal containers(UC) has identified exporting reports as a high-risk action and would like to require users to be logged into salesforce with their active directory (AD) credentialswhen doing so. For all other uses of Salesforce, Users should be allowed to use AD credentials or salesforce credentials.What solution should be recommended to prevent exporting reports except when logged in using AD credentials while maintaining the ability to view reports when logged in with salesforce credentials?
column4: A. Use SAML Federated Authentication and Custom SAML jit provisioning to dynamically add or remove a permission set that grants the Export Reports permission. | B. Use SAML Federated Authentication, treat SAML sessions as high assurance, and raise the session level required for exporting reports. | C. Use SAML Federated Authentication and block access to reports when accesses through a standard assurance session. | D. Use SAML Federated Authentication with a login flow to dynamically add or remove a permission set that grants the export reports permission.
column6: SAML sessions can be treated as High Assurance in Salesforce. By raising the session security level required for the 'Export Reports' permission (via Session Security Settings), users authenticated via AD/SAML at High Assurance can export, while users logged in with standard Salesforce credentials (standard assurance) cannot. This is the cleanest solution without complex dynamic provisioning.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is looking to purchase a third-party applicationas an Identity Provider. UC is looking to develop a business case for the purchase in general and has enlisted an Architect for advice. Which two capabilities of an Identity Provider should the Architect detail to help strengthen the business case?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. The Identity Provider can authenticate multiple applications. | B. The Identity Provider can authenticate multiple social media accounts. | C. The Identity provider can store credentials for multiple applications. | D. The Identity Provider can centralize enterprise password policy.
column6: Key business-case capabilities of an Identity Provider (IdP): (A) An IdP can authenticate users to multiple applications using SSO, reducing password fatigue and improving adoption; (D) An IdP centralizes enterprise password policies, enforcing consistent password complexity, rotation, and lockout rules across all connected apps. Options B and C are not primary IdP value propositions.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) uses a Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML)-based Identity Provider (idP) to authenticate employees to all systems. The IdP authenticates users against a Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) directory and has access to user information. NTO wants to minimize Salesforce license usage since only a small percentage of users need Salesforce.What is recommended to ensure new employees have immediate access to Salesforce using their current IdP?
column4: A. Install Salesforce Identity Connect to automatically provision new users in Salesforce the first time they attempt to login. | B. Build an integration that queries LDAP periodically and creates new active users in Salesforce. | C. Configure Just-in-Time provisioning using SAML attributes to create new Salesforce users as necessary when a new user attempts to login to Salesforce. | D. Build an integration that queries LDAP and creates new inactive users in Salesforce and use a login flow to activate the user atfirst login.
column6: JIT (Just-in-Time) provisioning uses attributes in SAML assertions to automatically create or update Salesforce user records at login time. This eliminates the need to pre-provision users and minimizes license usage because Salesforce accounts are only created when users actually attempt to log in. Identity Connect (A) and periodic LDAP sync (B) create users proactively, which doesn't minimize license usage.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is building a customer community and will allow customers to authenticate using Facebook credentials. The First time the user authenticating using Facebook, UC would like a customer account created automatically in their accounting system. The accounting system has a web service accessible to Salesforce for the creation of accounts. How can the Architect meet these requirements?
column4: A. Create a custom application on Heroku that manages the sign-on process from Facebook. | B. Use JIT Provisioning to automatically create the account in the accounting system. | C. Add an Apex callout in the registration handler of the authorization provider. | D. Use OAuth JWT flow to pass the data fromSalesforce to the Accounting System.
column6: When using a Social Sign-On (authentication provider) flow in Salesforce, the registration handler Apex class controls user creation/update. To perform an additional callout to an external accounting system when a user first registers, the architect should add an Apex callout inside the createUser method of the registration handler. Login flows (B) and JIT (B) don't apply here; OAuth JWT (D) is unrelated to registration.

column3: Universal Container's (UC) identity architect needs to recommend a license type for their new Experience Cloud site that will be used by external partners (delivery providers) for reviewing and updating their accounts, downloading files provided by UC and obtaining scheduled pickup dates from their calendar.UC is using their Salesforce production org as the identity provider for these users and the expected number of individual users is 2.5 million with 13.5 million unique logins per month.Which of the following license types should be used to meet the requirement?
column4: A. External Apps License | B. Partner CommunityLicense | C. Partner Community Login License | D. Customer Community plus Login License
column6: With 2.5 million users and 13.5 million logins per month (~5.4 logins/user/month), a login-based license is cost-effective. Partner Community Login License is designed for partner users who need to manage accounts, download files, and access calendar data. External Apps Login License would apply to simpler external app access; Customer Community licenses are for B2C customers, not delivery partners.

column3: An Identity architect works for a multinational, multi-brand organization. As they work with the organization to understand their Customer Identity and Access Management requirements, the identity architect learns that the brand experience is different for each of the customer's sub-brands and each of these branded experiences must be carried through the login experience depending on which sub-brand the user is logging into.Which solution should the architect recommend to support scalability and reduce maintenance costs, if the organization has more than 150sub-brands?
column4: A. Assign each sub-brand a unique Experience ID and use the Experience ID to dynamically brand the login experience. | B. Use Audiences to customize the login experience for each sub-brand and pass an audience ID to the community during the OAuthand Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) flows. | C. Create a community subdomain for each sub-brand and customize the look and feel of the Login page for each community subdomain to match the brand. | D. Create a separate Salesforce org for each sub-brand so that each sub-brand has complete control over the user experience.
column6: Experience ID (expid) is a URL parameter that can be passed in OAuth and SAML flows to dynamically apply branding to the Salesforce login page. With 150+ sub-brands, a single community can serve all brands by dynamically rendering the correct branding based on the Experience ID parameter, making this the most scalable and low-maintenance approach compared to creating separate orgs or communities per brand.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters recently acquired a company. Each company will retain its Identity Provider (IdP).Both companies rely extensively on Salesforce processes that send emails to users to take specific actions in Salesforce.How should the combined companys' employees collaborate in a single Salesforce org, yet authenticate to the appropriate IdP?
column4: A. Configure unique MyDomains for each company and have generated links use the appropriate MyDomain in the URL. | B. Have generated links append a querystring parameter indicating the IdP. The login service will redirect to the appropriate IdP. | C. Have generated links be prefixed with the appropriate IdP URL to invoke an IdP-initiated Security Assertion Markup Language flow when clicked. | D. Enable each IdP as a login option in the MyDomain Authentication Service settings. Users will then click onthe appropriate IdP button.
column6: After an acquisition where each company keeps its own IdP, the combined Salesforce org can configure both IdPs as login options in My Domain's Authentication Service settings. Users see buttons for each IdP on the login page and click the appropriate one. This is the simplest approach without requiring URL manipulation or custom code.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) is planning to implement a community for its customersusing Salesforce Experience Cloud. Customers are not able to self-register. NTO would like to have customers set their own passwords when provided access to the community.Which two recommendations should an identity architect make to fulfill this requirement?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Add customers as contacts and add them to Experience Cloud site. | B. Enable Welcome emails while configuring the Experience Cloud site. | C. Allow Password reset using the API to update Experience Cloud site membership. | D. Use Login Flows to allow users to reset password in Experience Cloud site.
column6: Since NTO customers cannot self-register but need to set their own passwords when given access: (B) Enabling Welcome Emails sends a password-set link to new users so they can establish their own password; (D) Allowing Password Reset via API lets administrators programmatically trigger password resets when updating community membership. Adding contacts (A) alone doesn't set passwords, and Login Flows (C) don't address the password-setting requirement. [CORRECTED from C,D to B,D]

column3: Universal containers (UC) has a mobile application that it wants to deploy toall of its salesforce users, including customer Community users. UC would like to minimize the administration overhead, which two items should an architect recommend? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Enable the "Refresh Tokens is valid until revoked " setting in the Connected App. | B. Enable the "Enforce Ip restrictions" settings in the connected App. | C. Enable the "All users may self-authorize" setting in the Connected App. | D. Enable the "High Assurance session required" setting in the Connected App.
column6: To minimize administration overhead for a mobile app used by all Salesforce users including Community users: (A) 'Refresh Tokens is valid until revoked' ensures users stay logged in indefinitely without re-authorizing; (C) 'All users may self-authorize' removes the need for admin approval for each user. Enforcing IP restrictions (B) would break mobile access, and High Assurance (D) adds complexity.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has a custom, internal-only, mobile billing application for users who are commonly out of the office. The app is configured asa connected App in Salesforce. Due to the nature of this app, UC would like to take the appropriate measures to properly secure access to the app. Which two are recommendations to make the UC? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Disallow the use of Single Sign-on for anyusers of the mobile app. | B. Require High Assurance sessions in order to use the Connected App. | C. Set Login IP Ranges to the internal network for all of the app users Profiles. | D. Use Google Authenticator as an additional part of the login process
column6: For a sensitive internal mobile billing app used outside the office: (B) Requiring High Assurance sessions adds an additional authentication step (like MFA) for app access; (D) Using Google Authenticator (or any TOTP app) provides a second factor, implementing MFA for the sensitive application. Disallowing SSO (A) increases friction unnecessarily, and login IP ranges (C) would block legitimate mobile access.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is building a custom Innovation platform on their Salesforce instance. The Innovation platform will be written completely in Apex and Visualforce and will use custom objects to store the Data. UC would like all users to be able to access the system without having to log in with Salesforce credentials. UC will utilize a third-party idp using SAML SSO. What is the optimal Salesforce licence type for all of the UC employees?
column4: A. Identity Licence. | B. Salesforce Licence. | C. External Identity Licence. | D. Salesforce Platform Licence.
column6: Since the Innovation platform is built entirely on Salesforce (Apex + Visualforce + custom objects), it uses standard Salesforce features. Salesforce Platform License provides access to custom Force.com apps and standard functionality without a full CRM license. It is the most cost-effective option for employees who only need the custom app and SSO access, not full Sales/Service Cloud.

column3: A client is planning to rollout multi-factor authentication (MFA) to its internal employees and wants to understand which authentication and verification methods meet the Salesforce criteria for secure authentication.Which three functions meet the Salesforce criteria for secure mfa?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. username and password + SMS passcode | B. Username and password + security key | C. Third-party single sign-on with Mobile Authenticator app | D. Certificate-based Authentication | E. Lightning Login
column6: Salesforce's MFA criteria require a strong second factor beyond username/password: (B) A hardware security key (FIDO2/WebAuthn) is phishing-resistant and Salesforce-approved; (C) A third-party SSO provider combined with a mobile authenticator app meets MFA requirements; (E) Lightning Login (push notification + PIN) is a Salesforce-approved passwordless MFA method. SMS passcode (A) does not meet Salesforce's MFA criteria. Certificate-based authentication (D) is a single factor.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has an Experience Cloud site (Customer Community) where customers can authenticate andplace orders, view the status of orders, etc. UC allows guest checkout. How can a guest register using data previously collected during order placement?
column4: A. Enable Security Assertion Markup Language Sign-On and use a login flow to collect only order detailsto retrieve customer data. | B. Enable Facebook as an authentication provider and use a registration handler to collect only order details to retrieve customer data. | C. Use a Connected App Handler Apex Plugin class to collect only order details to retrievecustomer data. | D. Enable self-registration and customize a self-registration page to collect only order details to retrieve customer data.
column6: To allow a guest user who placed an order to register using their order data, UC should enable self-registration and customize the self-registration page to collect only the order details (e.g., order number + email) and use that data to pre-populate the registration form or match the existing customer record. This leverages existing guest checkout data for a seamless registration experience.

column3: Universal Containers want users to be able to log in to the Salesforce mobile app with their Active Directory password. Employees are unable to use mobile VPN.Which two options should an identity architect recommend to meet therequirement?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Active Directory Password Sync Plugin | B. Configure Cloud Provider Load Balancer | C. Salesforce Trigger & Field on Contact Object | D. Salesforce Identity Connect
column6: To allow Salesforce mobile app login using AD passwords without VPN: (A) Active Directory Password Sync Plugin synchronizes AD passwords to Salesforce, allowing users to log in with their AD credentials directly; (D) Salesforce Identity Connect synchronizes users from AD and enables SAML-based SSO. Both solutions work without requiring mobile VPN access to the corporate network.

column3: A group of userstry to access one of universal containers connected apps and receive the following error message: "Failed : Not approved for access". what is most likely to cause of the issue?
column4: A. The use of high assurance sessions are required for the connected App. | B. The users do not have the correct permission set assigned to them. | C. The connected App setting "All users may self-authorize" is enabled. | D. The salesforce administrators have revoked the Oauth authorization.
column6: The 'Failed: Not approved for access' error in a Connected App means the user does not have the required permission to access the app. This typically means the app is configured to require admin-approved access (not 'All users may self-authorize'), and the user doesn't have the correct permission set assigned. The solution is to assign the appropriate permission set that grants access to the Connected App.

column3: A Salesforce customer is implementing Sales Cloud and a custom pricing application for its call center agents.An Enterprise single sign-on solution is used to authenticate and sign-in users to all applications. The customer has the following requirements:1. The development team has decided touse a Canvas app to expose the pricing application to agents.2. Agents should be able to access the Canvas app without needing to log in to the pricing application.Which two options should the identity architect consider to provide support for the Canvas app to initiate login for users?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Select "Enable as a Canvas Personal App" in the connected app settings. | B. Enable OAuth settings in the connected app with required OAuth scopes for the pricing application. | C. Configure the Canvas app as a connected app and set Admin-approved users as pre-authorized. | D. Enable SAML in the connected app and Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) Initiation Method as Service Provider Initiated.
column6: To provide Canvas app support for user login without requiring separate login to the pricing application: (C) Configuring the Canvas app as a Connected App with Admin-approved users pre-authorized means users are automatically authenticated; (D) Enabling SAML in the Connected App with SP-Initiated flow allows the Canvas app to initiate SSO through the enterprise SSO solution. OAuth scopes alone (B) don't handle Canvas authentication, and 'Personal App' (A) doesn't auto-authorize.

column3: An identity architect's client has a homegrown identity provider (IdP). Salesforce is used as the service provider (SP). The head of ITis worried that during a SP initiated single sign-on (SSO), the Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) request content will be altered.What should the identity architect recommend to make sure that there is additional trust between the SP and the IdP?
column4: A. Ensure that there is an HTTPS connection between IDP and SP. | B. Ensure that on the SSO settings page, the "Request Signing Certificate" field has a self-signed certificate. | C. Ensure that the Issuer and Assertion Consumer service (ACS) URL is propertyconfigured between SP and IDP. | D. Encrypt the SAML Request using certification authority (CA) signed certificate and decrypt on IdP.
column6: To protect against SAML request tampering during SP-initiated SSO, the best approach is to encrypt the SAML request using a CA-signed certificate (Salesforce's certificate for encryption) and have the IdP decrypt it. This ensures confidentiality and integrity of the SAML request in transit. Digitally signing the request also prevents tampering and the IdP's certificate is used to sign from the SP side.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has an e-commerce website where customers can buy products, make payments, and manage their accounts. UC decides tobuild a Customer Community on Salesforce and wants to allow the customers to access the community from their accounts without logging in again. UC decides to implement an SP-initiated SSO using a SAML-compliant Idp. In this scenario where Salesforce is theService Provider, which two activities must be performed in Salesforce to make SP-initiated SSO work? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Configure SAML SSO settings. | B. Create a Connected App. | C. Configure Delegated Authentication. | D. Set up My Domain.
column6: SP-initiated SSO with Salesforce as the Service Provider requires: (A) Configure SAML SSO settings to define the IdP, ACS URL, issuer, and certificate; (D) Set up My Domain, which is a mandatory prerequisite for SP-initiated SSO — without My Domain, Salesforce cannot initiate the SAML flow. Delegated Authentication (C) and Connected Apps (B) are not needed for SAML SSO.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has decided to replace the homegrown customer portalwith Salesforce Experience Cloud. UC will continue to use its third-party single sign-on (SSO) solution that stores all of its customer and partner credentials.The first time a customer logs in to the Experience Cloud site through SSO, a user record needsto be created automatically.Which solution should an identity architect recommend in order to automatically provision users in Salesforce upon login?
column4: A. Just-in-Time (JIT) provisioning | B. Custom middleware and web services | C. Custom login flow and Apex handler | D. Third-party AppExchange solution
column6: JIT (Just-in-Time) provisioning automatically creates Salesforce user records the first time a user logs in via SSO. SAML assertions from the third-party SSO solution contain user attributes that Salesforce uses to create the user record on the fly. This requires no custom code (unlike middleware or login flows) and is the recommended out-of-the-box solution.

column3: Universal Containers is implementing Salesforce Identity to broker authentication from its enterprise single sign-on (SSO) solution through Salesforce to third-party applications using SAML.What role does Salesforce Identity play in its relationship with the enterprise SSO system?
column4: A. Identity Provider (IdP) | B. Resource Server | C. Service Provider (SP) | D. Client Application
column6: In this scenario, Salesforce Identity is positioned between the enterprise SSO (IdP) and third-party SAML applications. Salesforce receives authentication from the enterprise SSO (acting as SP) and then issues SAML assertions to third-party apps (acting as IdP). Relative to the enterprise SSO system, Salesforce Identity plays the role of Service Provider (SP). It is both SP to the enterprise SSO and IdP to the third-party apps.

column3: How should an Architect force user to authenticate with Two-factor Authentication (2FA) for Salesforce only when not connected to an internal company network?
column4: A. Use Custom Login Flows with Apex to detect the user's IP address and prompt for 2FA if needed. | B. Add the list of company's network IP addresses to the Login Range list under 2FA Setup. | C. Use an Apex Trigger on the UserLogin object to detect the user's IP address and prompt for 2FA if needed. | D. Apply the "Two-factor Authentication for User Interface Logins" permission and Login IP Ranges for all Profiles.
column6: To enforce 2FA only for users outside the corporate network, a custom Login Flow with Apex is the correct approach. The Apex code checks the user's IP address at login time; if the IP is outside trusted corporate ranges, it triggers the 2FA step. This is more flexible than static IP range configurations on profiles, which would block access rather than prompt for 2FA.

column3: A third-party app provider would like to have users provisioned via a service endpoint before users access their app from Salesforce.What should an identity architect recommend to configure the requirement with limited changes to the third- party app?
column4: A. Use a connected app with user provisioning flow. | B. Create Canvas app in Salesforce for third-party app to provision users. | C. Redirect users to the third-party app for registration. | D. Use Salesforce identity with Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML)for provisioning users.
column6: Using a Connected App with User Provisioning Flow allows Salesforce to call the third-party app's service endpoint to provision users automatically when they are assigned to the connected app. This requires minimal changes to the third-party app (just exposing a provisioning endpoint) and leverages Salesforce's built-in user provisioning framework.

column3: An Identity and Access Management (IAM) architect is tasked with unifying multiple B2C Commerce sites and an Experience Cloud community with a single identity. The solution needs to support more than 1,000 logins per minute.What should the IAM do to fulfill this requirement?
column4: A. Configure both the community and the commerce sites as OAuth2 RPs (relying party) withan external identity provider. | B. Configure community as a Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) identity provider and enable Just-in-Time Provisioning to B2C Commerce. | C. Create a default account for capturing all ecommerce contacts registered on thecommunity because person Account is not supported for this case. | D. Confirm performance considerations with Salesforce Customer Support due to high peaks.
column6: To unify multiple B2C Commerce sites and an Experience Cloud community with a single identity supporting 1,000+ logins/minute, configure all sites as OAuth 2.0 Relying Parties (RPs) with an external enterprise-grade IdP. This architecture centralizes authentication, scales to high login volumes, and avoids Salesforce-specific bottlenecks. Using Salesforce as the IdP for B2C Commerce may not scale as well.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) wants to provide single sign-on (SSO) for a business-to-consumer (B2C) application using Salesforce Identity.Which Salesforce license should UC utilize to implement this use case?
column4: A. Identity Only | B. Salesforce Platform | C. External Identity | D. Partner Community
column6: External Identity license is designed for B2C use cases where Salesforce Identity is used to authenticate and manage consumer users. It provides identity functionality (login, registration, SSO) at a lower cost than Community licenses. Identity Only license is similar but intended for employee SSO scenarios; Partner Community is for B2B partner users.

column3: Universal containers (UC) wants to implement a partner community. As part of their implementation, UC would like to modify both the Forgot password and change password experience with custom branding for their partner community users. Which 2 actions should an architect recommend to UC? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Build a community builder page for the change password experience and Custom Visualforce page for the Forgot password experience. | B. Build acustom visualforce page for both the change password and Forgot password experiences. | C. Build a custom visualforce page for the change password experience and a community builder page for the Forgot password experience. | D. Build a community builder page for both the change password and Forgot password experiences.
column6: For a partner community, the Change Password page can be built as a custom Visualforce page (managed in Setup). The Forgot Password experience is handled via the community's Experience Builder login page. Therefore: (B) Custom Visualforce for both Change Password and Forgot Password is valid; (C) Custom Visualforce for Change Password with a community builder page for Forgot Password is also valid as Experience Builder supports Forgot Password customization.

column3: A global company's Salesforce Identity Architect is reviewing its Salesforce production org login history and is seeing some intermittent Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML SSO) 'Replay Detected and Assertion Invalid' login errors.Which two issues would cause these errors?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. The subject element ismissing from the assertion sent to salesforce. | B. The certificate loaded into SSO configuration does not match the certificate used by the IdP. | C. The current time setting of the company's identity provider (IdP) and Salesforce platform is out of sync by more than eight minutes. | D. The assertion sent to 5alesforce contains an assertion ID previously used.
column6: 'Replay Detected' errors occur when the same SAML assertion ID is submitted more than once. 'Assertion Invalid' errors often occur when the assertion timestamp is outside the acceptable window (typically ±5 minutes). Therefore: (C) Clock skew greater than 8 minutes (Salesforce's tolerance) causes time-based assertion failures; (D) Reuse of an assertion ID (replay attack detection) causes the 'Replay Detected' error.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) operates in Asia, Europe and North America regions. There is one Salesforce org for each region. UC is implementing Customer 360 in Salesforce and has procured External Identity and Customer Community licenses in all orgs.Customers of UC use Community to track orders and create inquiries. Customers also tend to move across regions frequently.What should an identity architect recommend to optimize license usage and reduce maintenance overhead?
column4: A. Merge three orgs into one instance of Salesforce. This will no longer require maintaining three separate copies of the same customer. | B. Delete contact/account records and deactivate user if user moves from a specific region; Sync will no longer be required. | C. Contacts are required since Community access needs to be enabled. Maintenance is a necessary overhead that must be handled via data integration. | D. Enable Contactless User in all orgs and downgrade users from Experience Cloud license to External Identity license once users have moved out of that region.
column6: Contactless Users (users without a Contact record) can hold External Identity licenses. When users move between regions, their active community license can be downgraded to External Identity (which is cheaper) in orgs they no longer actively use, while still keeping them as contactless users. This optimizes license usage and reduces the overhead of maintaining contact records in every org.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) wants its users to access Salesforce and other SSO-enabled applications from a custom web page that UC magnets. UC wants its users to use the same set of credentials to access each of the applications. what SAML SSO flow should an Architect recommend for UC?
column4: A. SP-Initiated with Deep Linking | B. SP-Initiated | C. IdP-Initiated | D. User-Agent
column6: IdP-initiated SSO is the correct flow when users start from a custom web page that UC manages (the IdP). The user authenticates at the IdP (UC's portal) first, then the IdP generates a SAML assertion and posts it to each SP (Salesforce and other apps). This enables a single login from UC's web page to access all SSO-enabled applications.

column3: What information does the 'Relaystate' parameter contain in sp-Initiated Single Sign-on?
column4: A. Referenceto a URL redirect parameter at the identity provider. | B. Reference to a URL redirect parameter at the service provider. | C. Reference to the login address URL of the service provider. | D. Reference to the login address URL of the identity Provider.
column6: In SP-initiated SSO, the RelayState parameter contains a URL or reference to a URL at the Service Provider that the user should be redirected to after successful authentication. It preserves the user's intended destination (deep link) at the SP. It does NOT refer to the IdP's URLs.

column3: Universal containers (UC) has implemented SAML -based single Sign-on for their salesforce application. UC is using PingFederate as the Identity provider. To access salesforce, Users usually navigate to a bookmarked link to my domain URL. What type of single Sign-on is this?
column4: A. Sp-Initiated | B. IDP-initiated with deep linking | C. IDP-initiated | D. Web server flow.
column6: When users navigate to a bookmarked My Domain URL in Salesforce (the Service Provider), Salesforce detects that SSO is configured and redirects users to the IdP login page. This is SP-initiated SSO — the flow is initiated by the Service Provider (Salesforce/My Domain URL). IdP-initiated SSO starts from the IdP's login portal.

column3: How should an Architect automatically redirect users to the login page of the external Identity provider when using an SP-Initiated SAML flow with Salesforce as a Service Provider?
column4: A. Use visualforce as the landing page for My Domain to redirect users to the Identity Provider login Page. | B. Enable the Redirect to the Identity Provider setting under Authentication Services on the My domain Configuration. | C. Remove the Login page from the list of Authentication Services on the My Domain configuration. | D. Set the Identity Provider as default and enable the Redirect to the Identity Provider setting on the SAML Configuration.
column6: To automatically redirect users to the IdP login page during SP-initiated SAML SSO, you must set the IdP as the default and enable 'Redirect to Identity Provider' in the SAML SSO Configuration settings in Salesforce. This tells My Domain to automatically redirect users to the configured IdP without showing the Salesforce login page.

column3: An Architect needs to advise the team that manages the Identity Provider how to differentiate Salesforce from other Service Providers. What SAML SSO setting in Salesforce provides this capability?
column4: A. IdentityProvider Login URL. | B. Issuer. | C. Entity Id | D. SAML Identity Location.
column6: The Entity ID (also called Audience URI) is a unique identifier for the Service Provider (Salesforce) in SAML configuration. It allows the IdP to distinguish between multiple Service Providers it serves. The SAML Issuer (B) identifies the IdP, not the SP.

column3: Which two capabilities does My Domain enable in the context of a SAML SSOconfiguration? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. App Launcher | B. Resource deep linking | C. SSO from Salesforce Mobile App | D. Login Forensics
column6: My Domain enables two key SSO capabilities: (B) Deep linking — users can be redirected to a specific Salesforce resource (not just the home page) after SSO authentication because My Domain provides a stable URL; (C) SSO from Salesforce Mobile App — the Salesforce mobile app requires a My Domain URL to initiate SSO flows. App Launcher (A) is an IdP feature, and Login Forensics (D) is an audit tool, not SSO-specific.

column3: Universal containers (UC) would like to enable self - registration for their salesforce partner community users.UC wants to capture some custom data elements from the partner user,and based on these data elements, wants to assign the appropriate profile and account values. Which two actions should the architect recommend to UC? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Modify the communitiesselfregcontroller to assign the profile and account. | B. Modify the selfregistration trigger to assign profile and account. | C. Configure registration for communities to use a custom visualforce page. | D. Configure registration for communities to use a custom apex controller.
column6: For partner community self-registration with custom data and dynamic profile/account assignment: (A) Modify the CommunitiesSelfRegController (the standard Apex controller) to read custom data fields and assign the appropriate profile and account based on business logic; (C) Configure registration to use a custom Visualforce page to capture the custom data elements. A trigger (B) fires after DML and is too late; a custom Apex controller alone (D) without a VF page isn't sufficient.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) wants to use Salesforce for sales orders and a legacy of system for order fulfillment. The legacy system must update the status of orders in 65* Salesforce in real time as they are fulfilled. UC decides to use OAuth for connecting the legacy system to Salesforce. What OAuth flow should be considered that doesn't require storing credentials, client secret or refresh tokens?
column4: A. Web Server flow | B. JWT Bearer Token flow | C. Username-Password flow | D. User Agent flow
column6: JWT Bearer Token flow is ideal for server-to-server integration (legacy system to Salesforce) where: no user interaction is required, no client secret needs to be stored, and no refresh token is needed. The legacy system signs a JWT with a private key and exchanges it for an access token. Username-Password (C) requires storing credentials; Web Server (A) and User-Agent (D) require user interaction.

column3: A university is planning to set up an identity solution for its alumni. A third-party identity provider will be used for single sign-on Salesforce will be the system of records. Users are getting error messages when logging in.Which Salesforce feature should be used to debug theissue?
column4: A. View Setup Audit Trail | B. Login History | C. Apex Exception Email | D. Debug Logs
column6: Login History in Salesforce provides detailed logs of authentication attempts including SSO login events, SAML assertion details, and error messages. It is the primary tool for debugging SAML SSO errors because it shows exactly what went wrong (e.g., invalid assertion, invalid issuer, certificate mismatch) during each login attempt.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) wants to build a few applications that leverage the Salesforce REST API. UC has asked its Architect to describe how the API calls will be authenticated to a specific user. Which two mechanisms can the Architect provide? Choose 2 Answers
column4: A. Authentication Token | B. Session ID | C. Refresh Token | D. Access Token
column6: Salesforce REST API calls are authenticated on behalf of a specific user using: (B) Session ID — obtained via SOAP login() call or from an existing authenticated Salesforce session; (D) Access Token — obtained via OAuth 2.0 flows and included in the Authorization header. Refresh Tokens (C) are used to obtain new access tokens but don't directly authenticate API calls. Authentication Token (A) is not a standard Salesforce mechanism. [CORRECTED from C,D to B,D]

column3: A public sector agency is setting up an identity solution for its citizens using a Community built on Experience Cloud and requires the new user registration functionality to capture first name, last name, and phone number. The phone number will be used for identity verification.Which feature should an identity architect recommend to meet therequirements?
column4: A. Integrate with social websites (Facebook, Linkedin. Twitter) | B. Use an external Identity Provider | C. Create a custom Lightning Web Component | D. Use Login Discovery
column6: Login Discovery allows custom user identification and login flows without requiring users to know their Salesforce username. It supports phone number-based identification and redirects to appropriate verification methods. For a public sector use case requiring first name, last name, and phone number for identity verification, Login Discovery with a custom handler is the recommended approach.

column3: Which twosecurity risks can be mitigated by enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) in Salesforce?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Users leaving laptops unattended and not logging out of Salesforce. | B. Users accessing Salesforce from a public Wi-Fi access point. | C. Users choosing passwords that are the same as their Facebook password. | D. Users creating simple-to-guess password reset questions.
column6: 2FA mitigates: (B) Public Wi-Fi risks — even if credentials are intercepted on an unsecured network, the second factor prevents unauthorized access; (C) Reused passwords from social media — even if the Salesforce password is compromised (same as Facebook), the attacker needs the second factor. 2FA does NOT prevent users from leaving laptops unattended (A) or from creating weak security questions (D).

column3: Universal Containers (UC) employees have Salesforce access from restricted IP ranges only, to protect against unauthorized access. UC wants to roll out the Salesforce1 mobile app and make it accessible from any location. Which two options should an Architect recommend? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Relax the IP restriction with a second factor in the Connect App settings for Salesforce1 mobile app. | B. Remove existing restrictions on IP ranges for all types of user access. | C. Relax the IP restrictions in the Connect App settings for the Salesforce1 mobile app. | D. Use Login Flow to bypass IP range restriction for the mobile app.
column6: To allow the Salesforce1 mobile app from any location while maintaining restricted IP ranges for other access: (A) 'Relax IP restriction with second factor' in the Connected App requires users outside trusted IPs to provide a second factor (MFA), balancing security and mobility; (C) 'Relax IP restrictions' in the Connected App settings bypasses IP restrictions specifically for mobile access. Both together provide flexible options.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) utilizesa third-party cloud solution for an employee portal. NTO also owns Salesforce Service Cloud and would like employees to be able to login to Salesforce with their third-party portal credentials for a seamless experience. The third-party employee portal onlysupports OAuth.What should an identity architect recommend to enable single sign-on (SSO) between the portal and Salesforce?
column4: A. Configure SSO to use the third-party portal as an identity provider. | B. Create a custom external authentication provider. | C. Add the third-party portal as a connected app. | D. Configure Salesforce for Delegated Authentication.
column6: Since the third-party portal only supports OAuth (not SAML), Salesforce cannot use SAML federation. Instead, Salesforce can act as an OAuth Service Provider by configuring an Authentication Provider that uses the third-party portal as the OAuth-based identity provider, enabling SSO through OAuth flows. Delegated Authentication (D) requires a SOAP web service, not OAuth.

column3: Universal Containers has multiple Salesforce instances where users receive emails from different instances.Users should be logged into the correct Salesforce instance authenticated by their IdP when clicking on an email link to a Salesforce record.What should be enabled in Salesforce as a prerequisite?
column4: A. My Domain | B. External Identity | C. Identity Provider | D. Multi-Factor Authentication
column6: My Domain is required so that email links from different Salesforce instances contain the correct org-specific domain. When a user clicks an email link, the My Domain URL directs them to the correct Salesforce instance, and the SSO flow is triggered against the appropriate IdP. Without My Domain, deep linking with SSO doesn't work correctly.

column3: IT security at Universal Containers (UC) is concerned about recent phishing scams targeting its users and wants to add additional layers of login protection. What should an Architect recommend to address the issue?
column4: A. Use the Salesforce Authenticator mobile app with two-step verification | B. Lock sessions to the IP address from which they originated. | C. Increase Password complexity requirements in Salesforce. | D. Implement Single Sign-on using a corporate Identity store.
column6: Salesforce Authenticator provides push notification-based two-step verification (2FA) that protects against phishing by requiring approval on a separate trusted device. Even if a user's credentials are stolen via phishing, the attacker cannot log in without access to the user's mobile device. This is more effective than password complexity (C) or SSO (D) alone against active phishing attacks.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) has an off-boarding process where a terminated employee is first disabled in the Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) directory, then requests are sent to the various application support teams to finish user deactivations. A terminated employee recently was able to login to NTO's Salesforce instance 24 hours after termination, even though the user was disabled in the corporate LDAP directory.What should an identity architect recommend to prevent this from happening in the future?
column4: A. Create a Just-in-Time provisioning registration handler to ensure users are deactivated in Salesforce as they are disabled inLDAP. | B. Configure an authentication provider to delegate authentication to the LDAP directory. | C. use a login flow to make a callout to the LDAP directory before authenticating the user to Salesforce. | D. Setup an identity provider (IdP) to authenticate users using LDAP, set up single sign-on to Salesforce and disable Login Form authentication.
column6: To ensure a terminated employee disabled in LDAP cannot log in to Salesforce, configure Delegated Authentication (B). With Delegated Authentication, Salesforce passes the user's credentials to the corporate LDAP/authentication service for validation on every login. If the user is disabled in LDAP, the authentication service returns 'false' and the user is denied access — in real time without waiting for a sync job.

column3: A global company has built an external application that uses data from its Salesforce org via an OAuth 2.0 authorization flow. Upon logout, the existing Salesforce OAuth token must be invalidated.Which action will accomplish this?
column4: A. Use a HTTP POST to request the refresh token for the current user. | B. Use a HTTP POST to the System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) endpoint, including the current OAuth token. | C. Use a HTTP POST to make a call to the revoke token endpoint. | D. Enable Single Logout with a secure logout URL.
column6: To invalidate an existing Salesforce OAuth token upon logout, the application must send an HTTP POST request to Salesforce's token revocation endpoint (/services/oauth2/revoke) with the token. This immediately invalidates the access token and any associated refresh token, preventing further API access.

column3: Universal containers (UC) would like to enable SAML-BASED SSO for asalesforce partner community. UC has an existing ldap identity store and a third-party portal. They would like to use the existing portal as the primary site these users' access, but also want to allow seamless access to the partner community. What SSO flow should an architect recommend?
column4: A. User-Agent | B. IDP-initiated | C. Sp-Initiated | D. Web server
column6: IdP-initiated SSO is the correct flow when the primary site is the third-party portal (acting as the IdP). Users authenticate at the portal first, then the portal generates a SAML assertion and sends it to the Salesforce partner community (SP). This provides seamless access from the existing portal without requiring the user to navigate to Salesforce first.

column3: A multinational industrial products manufacturer is planning to implement Salesforce CRMto manage their business. They have the following requirements:1. They plan to implement Partner communities to provide access to their partner network .2. They have operations in multiple countries and are planning to implement multiple Salesforce orgs.3. Some of their partners do business in multiple countries and will need information from multiple Salesforce communities.4. They would like to provide a single login for their partners.How should an Identity Architect solution this requirement with limited custom development?
column4: A. Create a partner login for the country of their operation and use SAML federation to provide access to other orgs. | B. Consolidate Partner related information in a single org and provide access through Salesforce community. | C. Allow partners to choose the Salesforce org they need information from and use login flows to authenticate access. | D. Register partners in one org and access information from other orgs using APIs.
column6: For multinational partners needing access to multiple country-specific Salesforce orgs with a single login and minimal custom development: register partners in their primary country's org, set that org as an IdP for the other orgs, and use SAML federation with JIT provisioning to grant access to other orgs. This leverages built-in Salesforce federation capabilities.

column3: An identity architect is setting up an integration between Salesforce and a third-party system. The third-party system needs to authenticate to Salesforce and then make API calls against the REST API.One of the requirements is that the solution needs to ensure the third party service providers connected app in Salesforce mini need for end user interaction and maximizes security.Which OAuth flow should be used to fulfill the requirement?
column4: A. JWT Bearer Flow | B. Web Server Flow | C. User Agent Flow | D. Username-Password Flow
column6: JWT Bearer Flow is ideal for server-to-server integration where: (1) no end-user interaction is required, (2) no client secret needs to be stored/transmitted, and (3) security is maximized via certificate-based authentication. The third-party system signs a JWT with a private certificate and exchanges it for a Salesforce access token, ensuring secure, non-interactive authentication.

column3: which three are features of federated Single Sign-on solutions? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. It federates credentials control toauthorized applications. | B. It establishes trust between Identity store and service provider. | C. It solves all identity and access management problems. | D. It improves affiliated applications adoption rates. | E. It enables quick and easy provisioning and deactivating of users.
column6: Features of federated SSO: (A) Credentials control is federated to the IdP (not stored in each app); (B) Trust relationships are established between IdP and each SP via certificates/metadata; (D) Application adoption improves because users don't need separate passwords. Federated SSO does not solve all IAM problems (C) and doesn't inherently make provisioning/deprovisioning quick and easy (E) — that requires separate tooling.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters is implementing a business-to-business (B2B) collaborationsite using Salesforce Experience Cloud. The partners will authenticate with an existing identity provider and the solution will utilize Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) to provide single sign-on to Salesforce. Delegated administration will be used in the Experience Cloud site to allow the partners to administer their users' access.How should a partner identity be provisioned in Salesforce for this solution?
column4: A. Create only a contact. | B. Create a contactless user. | C. Create a user and a related contact. | D. Create a person account.
column6: For B2B collaboration with SAML SSO and delegated administration, partner identities in Salesforce need both a User record (for authentication and Salesforce access) and a related Contact record (which represents the individual person in the partner Account and enables Experience Cloud access, role hierarchy, and data sharing). A contactless user (B) does not work for B2B partner communities requiring delegated admin.

column3: Universal containers(UC) wants to integrate a third-party reward calculation system with salesforce to calculate rewards. Rewards will be calculated on a schedule basis and update back into salesforce. The integration between Salesforce and the reward calculation system needs to be secure. Which are the recommended best practices for using Oauth flows in this scenario? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Oauth refresh token flow | B. Oauth SAML bearer assertion flow | C. OAuth JWT bearer token flow | D. Oauth Username-password flow
column6: For a scheduled server-to-server integration between Salesforce and a reward calculation system: (A) OAuth Refresh Token Flow maintains long-lived sessions by refreshing the access token without user interaction; (C) OAuth JWT Bearer Token Flow provides secure, non-interactive server-to-server authentication without storing credentials. SAML Bearer (B) requires an existing SAML SSO setup; Username-Password (D) requires storing credentials.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) built an integration for their employees to post, view, and vote for ideas in Salesforce from an internal Company portal. When ideas are posted in Salesforce, links to the ideas are created in the company portal pages as part of the integration process. The Company portal connects to Salesforce using OAuth. Everything is working fine, except when users click on links to existing ideas, they are always taken to the Ideas home page rather than the specific idea, after authorization.Which OAuth URL parameter can be used to retain the original requested page so that a user can be redirected correctly after OAuth authorization?
column4: A. Redirect_uri | B. State | C. Scope | D. Callback_uri
column6: In OAuth authorization flows, the 'state' parameter is used to pass arbitrary data through the authorization process. It can encode the user's original intended URL (the specific idea link), and after OAuth completes, the application reads the 'state' parameter in the callback to redirect the user to the correct page. The 'redirect_uri' (A) is where the OAuth callback goes, not where the user ends up. [CORRECTED from A to B]

column3: Universal containers (UC) is setting up Delegated Authentication to allow employees to log in using their corporate credentials. UC's security team is concerned about the risk of exposing the corporate login service on the Internet and has asked that a reliable trust mechanism be put in place between the loginservice and salesforce. What mechanism should an architect put in place to enable a trusted connection between the login services and salesforce?
column4: A. Include client ID and client secret in the login header callout. | B. Set up a proxy server for the login service in the DMZ. | C. Require the use of Salesforce security Tokens on password. | D. Enforce mutual Authentication between systems using SSL.
column6: To establish a trusted connection between Salesforce and the Delegated Authentication web service, mutual SSL (two-way SSL) authentication should be enforced. Both sides present certificates to authenticate each other, ensuring the service is called only from Salesforce and that Salesforce is talking to the legitimate login service. This protects the corporate login service from unauthorized calls.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is planning to add Wi-Fi enabled GPS tracking devices to its shipping containers so that the GPS coordinates data can be sent from the tracking device to its Salesforce production org via a custom API. The GPS devices have no direct user input or output capabilities.Which OAuth flow should the identity architect recommend to meet the requirement?
column4: A. OAuth 2.0 Asset Token Flow for Securing Connected Devices | B. OAuth 2.0 Username-Password Flow for Special Scenarios | C. OAuth 2.0 WebServer Flow for Web App Integration | D. OAuth 2.0 JWT Bearer Flow for Server-to-Server Integration
column6: OAuth 2.0 Asset Token Flow is specifically designed for IoT/connected devices that have no user input/output capabilities. Devices obtain a token tied to a digital asset (the device itself), not to a user session. This is the correct flow for GPS tracking devices that send data autonomously to Salesforce. JWT Bearer Flow (D) requires a user context; Device Flow requires user interaction.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) uses Salesforce for its customer service agents. UC has a proprietary system for order tracking which supports Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) based single sign-on. The VP of customer service wants to ensure only active Salesforce users should be able to access the order tracking system which is only visible within Salesforce.What should be done to fulfill the requirement?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Setup Salesforce as an identity provider (IdP) for order Tracking. | B. Set up the Corporate Identity store as an identity provider (IdP) for Order Tracking, | C. Customize Order Tracking to initiate a REST call to validate users in Salesforce after login. | D. Setup Order Tracking as a Canvas app in Salesforce to POST IdPinitiated SAML assertion.
column6: To ensure only active Salesforce users can access the order tracking system via SSO: (A) Set up Salesforce as the IdP for the order tracking system — when a user attempts SSO, Salesforce validates they are an active Salesforce user and generates a SAML assertion; (D) Set up the order tracking system as a Canvas app in Salesforce with IdP-initiated SAML — the assertion is generated and posted when the Canvas app loads within Salesforce.

column3: Containers (UC) has implemented SAML-based single Sign-on for their Salesforce application and is planning to provide access to Salesforce on mobile devices using the Salesforce1 mobile app. UC wants to ensure that Single Sign-on is used for accessing the Salesforce1 mobile App. Which two recommendations should the Architect make? Choose 2 Answers
column4: A. Configure the Embedded Web Browser to use My Domain URL. | B. Configure the Salesforce1 App to use the MY Domain URL. | C. Use the existing SAML-SSO flow along with User Agent Flow. | D. Use the existing SAML SSO flow along with Web Server Flow.
column6: For SSO with Salesforce1 Mobile App: (B) Configure the Salesforce1 App to use the My Domain URL — this ensures the mobile app hits the correct org URL and triggers the SSO flow; (C) Use the existing SAML SSO flow along with the User Agent Flow — the mobile app uses the User Agent (browser-based) OAuth flow in conjunction with SAML SSO for the initial authentication handshake.

column3: Which two considerations should be made whenimplementing Delegated Authentication?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. The authentication web service can include custom attributes. | B. It can be used to authenticate API clients and mobile apps. | C. It requires trusted IP ranges at the User Profile level. | D. Salesforce servers receive but do not validate a user's credentials. | E. Just-in-time Provisioning can be configured for new users.
column6: Delegated Authentication considerations: (B) It can authenticate API clients and mobile apps — not limited to browser-based logins; (E) JIT provisioning can be configured alongside Delegated Authentication — when a user successfully authenticates via the external service, JIT can create/update the Salesforce user. Trusted IP ranges (C) are configured at the org level, not user profile level, for Delegated Auth.

column3: A division of a Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) purchased Salesforce. NTO uses a third party identity provider (IdP) to validate user credentials against Its corporate Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) directory. NTO wants to help employees remember as passwords as possible.What should an identity architect recommend?
column4: A. Setup Salesforce as a Service Provider to the existing IdP. | B. SetupSalesforce as an IdP to authenticate against the LDAP directory. | C. Use Salesforce connect to synchronize LDAP passwords to Salesforce. | D. Setup Salesforce as an Authentication Provider to the existing IdP.
column6: To allow employees to use their existing LDAP/IdP credentials to log in to Salesforce (minimize passwords), set up Salesforce as a Service Provider (SP) to the existing third-party IdP. The IdP authenticates users against the LDAP directory and sends SAML assertions to Salesforce. This reduces passwords since employees use their existing IdP credentials.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has implemented SAML-based SSO solution for use with their multi-org Salesforce implementation, utilizing one ofthe the orgs as the Identity Provider. One user is reporting that they can log in to the Identity Provider org but get a generic SAML error message when accessing the other orgs.Which two considerations should the architect review to troubleshoot the issue? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. The Federation ID must be a valid Salesforce Username | B. The Federation ID must is case sensitive | C. The Federation ID must be in the form of an email address. | D. The Federation ID must be populated on the user record.
column6: When troubleshooting SAML SSO errors where one user cannot access other orgs: (B) Federation ID is case-sensitive — if the value in Salesforce doesn't exactly match the NameID in the SAML assertion (including case), SSO fails; (D) Federation ID must be populated on the user record — if it's blank, Salesforce cannot match the SAML assertion to a user. The Federation ID doesn't need to be in email format (C) or a valid Salesforce username (A).

column3: Which three are capabilities of SAML-based Federated authentication? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Trust relationships between Identity Provider and Service Provider are required. | B. SAML tokens can be in XML or JSON format and can be used interchangeably. | C. Web applications with no passwords are more secure and stronger against attacks. | D. Access tokens areused to access resources on the server once the user is authenticated. | E. Centralized federation provides single point of access, control and auditing.
column6: Capabilities of SAML-based Federated Authentication: (A) Trust relationships between IdP and SP are required — established via certificates/metadata exchange; (C) Web applications with no stored passwords are more secure and stronger against credential-based attacks; (E) Centralized federation provides single point for access control, auditing, and policy enforcement. SAML tokens are XML-based (B is wrong — they cannot be JSON). Access tokens (D) are an OAuth concept, not SAML.

column3: Universal containers (UC) wants to integrate a Web application with salesforce. The UC team hasimplemented the Oauth web-server Authentication flow for authentication process. Which two considerations should an architect point out to UC? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. The web application should be hosted on a secure server. | B. The web server must be able to protect consumer privacy | C. The flow involves passing the user credentials back and forth. | D. The flow will not provide an Oauth refresh token back to the server.
column6: Web Server OAuth Flow considerations: (A) The web application must be hosted on a secure server to protect the client secret and authorization code; (B) The web server must protect consumer secret/privacy because the client secret is stored server-side. The flow does NOT pass user credentials (C is wrong — that's Username-Password flow). The flow DOES return a refresh token (D is wrong).

column3: Universal containers (UC) has multiple salesforce orgs and would like to use a single identity provider to access all of their orgs. How should UC'S architect enable this behavior?
column4: A. Ensure that users have the same email value in their user records in all of UC's salesforce orgs. | B. Ensure the same username is allowed in multiple orgs by contacting salesforce support. | C. Ensure that users have the same Federation ID value in their user recordsin all of UC's salesforce orgs. | D. Ensure that users have the same alias value in their user records in all of UC's salesforce orgs.
column6: For a single IdP to authenticate users across multiple Salesforce orgs, the Federation ID on each user record in each org must match the NameID value sent in the SAML assertion. The Federation ID uniquely identifies the user across all orgs and must be the same value in all orgs for the same user. Email (A) or alias (D) fields are not used for SAML matching.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is building an authenticated Customer Community for its customers. UC does not want customer credentialsstored in Salesforce and is confident its customers would be willing to use their social media credentials to authenticate to the community. Which two actions should an Architect recommend UC to take?
column4: A. Use Delegated Authentication to call the Twitter login API to authenticate users. | B. Configure an Authentication Provider for LinkedIn Social Media Accounts. | C. Create a Custom Apex Registration Handler to handle new and existing users. | D. Configure SSO Settings For Facebook to serve as a SAML Identity Provider.
column6: To allow customers to use social media credentials without storing credentials in Salesforce: (B) Configure an Authentication Provider for LinkedIn (Salesforce has a predefined LinkedIn provider using OAuth 2.0); (C) Create a custom Apex Registration Handler to map social identity attributes to Salesforce user fields and handle new vs. existing user logic. Delegated Authentication (A) delegates username/password validation, not social login. Facebook doesn't act as a SAML IdP (D).

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has a Customer Community that uses Facebook for Authentication. UC would like to ensure that Changes in the Facebook profile are reflected on the appropriate Customer Community user:How can this requirement be met?
column4: A. Use the updateUser method on the registration Handler Class. | B. Develop a scheduled job that calls out to Facebook on anightly basis. | C. Use information in the signed Request that is received from facebook. | D. Use SAML Just-In-Time Provisioning between Facebook and Salesforce.
column6: The updateUser method in the Auth.RegistrationHandler interface is called every time an existing user logs in via the authentication provider. By implementing this method to retrieve updated profile data from Facebook and update the Salesforce user record, changes in the Facebook profile are automatically reflected in the Community user on each login.

column3: When designing a multi-branded Customer Identity and Access Management solution on the Salesforce Platform, how should an identity architect ensure a specific brand experience in Salesforce is presented?
column4: A. The Experience ID, which can be included in OAuth/Open ID flows and Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) flows as a URL parameter. | B. Provide a brand picker that the end user can use to select its sub-brand when they arrive on salesforce. | C. Add a custom parameter to the service provider's OAuth/SAML call and implement logic on its login page to apply branding based on the parameters value. | D. The Audience ID, which can be set in a shared cookie.
column6: The Experience ID (expid) is a URL parameter that can be included in OAuth, OpenID Connect, and SAML flows. When a user accesses a specific sub-brand URL that includes the expid parameter, Salesforce renders the login page with that brand's custom look and feel. This is the recommended approach for multi-branded CIAM on the Salesforce Platform.

column3: Universal Container's (UC) is using Salesforce Experience Cloud site for its containerwholesale business. The identity architect wants to an authentication provider for the new site.Which two options should be utilized in creating an authentication provider?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. A custom registration handler can be set. | B. A custom error URL can be set. | C. The default login user can be set. | D. The default authentication provider certificate can be set.
column6: When creating an Authentication Provider in Salesforce: (A) A custom registration handler (Apex class) can be specified to control how users are created or updated upon login; (B) A custom error URL can be configured to redirect users to a branded error page if authentication fails. The default login user (C) and default auth provider certificate (D) are not standard configuration options for authentication providers.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) wants to give customers the ability to submit and manage issues with their purchases. It is important for to give its customers the ability to login with their Facebook and Twitter credentials.Which two actions should an identity architect recommend to meet these requirements?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Create a custom external authentication provider for Facebook. | B. Configure a predefined authentication provider for Facebook. | C. Create a custom external authentication provider for Twitter. | D. Configure a predefined authentication provider for Twitter.
column6: Salesforce has predefined (out-of-the-box) authentication providers for Facebook and Twitter (and other major social platforms). For NTO's requirement: (B) Configure the predefined authentication provider for Facebook; (D) Configure the predefined authentication provider for Twitter. There is no need to build custom providers (A, C) for these major platforms.

column3: Universal containers (UC) built a customer Community for customers to buy products, review orders, and manage their accounts. UC has provided three different options for customers to log in to the customer Community: salesforce, Google, and Facebook. Which two role combinations are represented by the systems in the scenario? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Google is the service provider and Facebook is the identity provider | B. Salesforceis the service provider and Google is the identity provider | C. Facebook is the service provider and salesforce is the identity provider | D. Salesforce is the service provider and Facebook is the identity provider
column6: In this scenario, Salesforce's Customer Community is the application users access (Service Provider). Google and Facebook are external systems that authenticate users (Identity Providers). Therefore: (B) Salesforce is the SP and Google is the IdP; (D) Salesforce is the SP and Facebook is the IdP. Salesforce is not acting as an IdP here, and Google/Facebook are not service providers.

column3: Universal Containers built a custom mobile app fortheir field reps to create orders in Salesforce. OAuth is used for authenticating mobile users. The app is built in such a way that when a user session expires after Initial login, a new access token is obtained automatically without forcing the user to log in again. While that improved the field reps' productivity, UC realized that they need a "logout" feature.What should the logout function perform in this scenario, where user sessions are refreshed automatically?
column4: A. Invoke the revocation URL and pass the refresh token. | B. Clear out the client Id to stop auto session refresh. | C. Invoke the revocation URL and pass the access token. | D. Clear out all the tokens to stop auto session refresh.
column6: When using OAuth with automatic token refresh, the logout function must revoke the refresh token (not just the access token) to prevent the app from automatically obtaining new access tokens. Calling the revocation endpoint with the refresh token invalidates the entire OAuth session, including all associated access tokens. Revoking only the access token (C) would allow the refresh token to generate new ones.

column3: Under which scenario Web Server flow will be used?
column4: A. Used for web applications when server-side code needs to interact with APIS. | B. Used for server-side components when page needs to be rendered. | C. Used for mobile applications and testing legacy Integrations. | D. Used for verifying Access protected resources.
column6: Web Server Flow (OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant) is used for web applications where server-side code needs to interact with Salesforce APIs. It involves: user authenticating via browser, authorization code returned to server, server exchanging code for access+refresh tokens, server making API calls. The server stores the client secret securely. It is NOT used for mobile apps (User-Agent/Device flow) or IoT devices.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has aclassified information system that its call center team uses only when they are working on a case with a record type "Classified". They are only allowed to access the system when they own an open "Classified" case, and their access to the system is removedat all other times. They would like to implement SAML SSO with Salesforce as the Idp, and automatically allow or deny the staff's access to the classified information system based on whether they currently own an open "Classified" case record when they try to access the system using SSO. What is the recommended solution for automatically allowing or denying access to the classified information system based on the open "classified" case record criteria?
column4: A. Use Salesforce reports to identify users that currently own open "Classified" cases and should be granted access to the Classified information system. | B. Use Apex trigger on case to dynamically assign permission Sets that Grant access when a user is assigned with an open "Classified" case, and remove it when the case is closed. | C. Use Custom SAML JIT Provisioning to dynamically query the user's open "Classified" cases when attempting to access the classified information system. | D. Use a Common Connected App Handler using Apex to dynamically allow access tothe system based on whether the staff owns any open "Classified" Cases.
column6: Custom SAML JIT Provisioning with a custom Apex handler allows dynamic evaluation of user attributes at SSO time. When the user attempts SSO, the custom JIT handler can query the user's open 'Classified' cases in real time and return allow/deny based on the result. This is more dynamic than triggers (B) which fire on case changes but can't intercept an active SSO attempt in real time.

column3: Universal containers (UC) would like to enable SSO between their existing Active Directory infrastructure and salesforce. The it team prefers to manage all users in Active Directory and would like to avoid doing any initial setup of users in salesforce directly,including the correct assignment of profiles, roles and groups.Which two optimal solutions should UC use to provision users in salesforce? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Use the salesforce REST API to sync users from active directory to salesforce | B. Use an app exchange product to sync users from Active Directory to salesforce. | C. Use Active Directory Federation Services to sync users from active directory to salesforce. | D. Use Identity connect to sync users from Active Directory to salesforce
column6: For syncing users from Active Directory to Salesforce with profile/role/group assignments: (B) AppExchange products (like Okta, Azure AD Connect, or other AD sync tools) provide robust AD-to-Salesforce user sync with attribute mapping; (D) Salesforce Identity Connect specifically syncs users from Active Directory to Salesforce and supports profile, role, and group assignment based on AD group membership.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has an existing web application that itwould like to access from Salesforce without requiring users to re-authenticate. The web application is owned UC and the UC team that is responsible for it is willing to add new javascript code and/or libraries to the application. What implementation should an Architect recommend to UC?
column4: A. Create a Canvas app and use Signed Requests to authenticate the users. | B. Rewrite the web application as a set of Visualforce pages and Apex code. | C. Configure the web application as an item in the Salesforce App Launcher. | D. Add the web application as a ConnectedApp using OAuth User-Agent flow.
column6: Canvas app with Signed Requests allows Salesforce to authenticate users to an external web application without re-login. The Canvas framework sends a signed request (containing the user's identity and OAuth token) to the web application. The UC team can add JavaScript to verify and use the signed request for authentication. This requires minimal changes to the existing application.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) uses middleware to integrate multiple systems with Salesforce. UC has a strict, new requirement that usernames and passwords cannot be stored in any UC system. How can UC's middleware authenticate to Salesforce while adhering to this requirement?
column4: A. Create a Connected App that supports the JWT Bearer Token OAuth Flow. | B. Create a Connected App that supports the Refresh Token OAuth Flow | C. Create a Connected App that supports the Web Server OAuth Flow. | D. Create a Connected App that supports the User-Agent OAuth Flow.
column6: JWT Bearer Token Flow requires no stored username/password and no client secret transmission. The middleware authenticates using a digital certificate (private key) to sign JWTs. Salesforce verifies the signature using the uploaded public certificate. This is the only OAuth flow that doesn't require storing credentials or secrets in the middleware system.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has a Customer Community that uses Facebook for of authentication. UC would like to ensure that changes in the Facebook profile are 65. reflected on the appropriate Customer Community user. How can this requirement be met?
column4: A. Use SAML Just-In-Time Provisioning between Facebook and Salesforce. | B. Use information in the Signed Request that is received from Facebook. | C. Develop a scheduled job that calls out to Facebook on a nightly basis. | D. Use the update User () method on the Registration Handler class.
column6: The updateUser() method in the Apex Registration Handler class is called on every subsequent login of an existing user via the authentication provider (Facebook in this case). By implementing this method to fetch current profile data from Facebook's API and update the corresponding Salesforce Community user, profile changes are automatically synchronized on each login.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) wants its closed Won opportunities to be synced to a Data Warehouse in near real time. UC has implemented Outbound Message to enable near real-time data sync. UC wants to ensure that communication between Salesforce and Target System is Secure. What Certificate is sent along with the Outbound Message?
column4: A. The CA-SignedCertificate from the Certificate and Key Management menu. | B. The default Client Certificate from the Develop--> API Menu. | C.
