The default Client Certificate or a Certificate from Certificate and Key Management menu. | D. The Self-Signed Certificates from theCertificate & Key Management menu.
column6: Salesforce Outbound Messages are sent with the API Client Certificate configured in Setup > Certificate and Key Management. This can be either the default client certificate (from the Develop > API menu) or a custom certificate from Certificate and Key Management. The answer C is the most complete and accurate description. [CORRECTED from A to C]

column3: Universal containers (UC) is concerned that having a self-registration page will provide a means for "bots" or unintended audiences to create user records, thereby consuming licences and adding dirty data. Which two actions should UC take to prevent unauthorised form submissions during theself-registration process? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Use open-ended security questions and complex password requirements | B. Primarily use lookup and picklist fields on the self registration page. | C. Require a captcha at the end of the self-registration process. | D. Use hidden fields populated via java script events in the self-registration page.
column6: To prevent bots and unauthorized form submissions during self-registration: (C) CAPTCHA requires human interaction to complete the form, blocking automated bot submissions; (D) Hidden fields populated via JavaScript events (honeypot fields) can detect bots because bots typically fill all fields while humans won't interact with hidden fields. Open-ended questions (A) and picklist fields (B) don't prevent bot submissions.

column3: universal container plans to develop a custom mobile app for the sales team that will use salesforce for authentication and access management. The mobile app access needs to be restricted to only the sales team.What would be the recommended solution to grant mobile app access to sales users?
column4: A. Use a custom attribute on the user object to control access to the mobile app | B. Use connected apps Oauth policies to restrictmobile app access to authorized users. | C. Use the permission set license to assign the mobile app permission to sales users | D. Add a new identity provider to authenticate and authorize mobile users.
column6: Connected App OAuth policies allow administrators to restrict which users can access a connected app. By configuring the connected app to allow only 'Admin approved users' and assigning specific profiles or permission sets (for the sales team), access is automatically restricted to only sales users. Custom user attributes (A) would require custom code.

column3: Universal containers uses an Employee portal for their employees to collaborate. employees access the portal from their company's internal website via SSO. It is set up to work with Active Directory. What is the role of Active Directory in this scenario?
column4: A. Identity store | B. Authentication store | C. Identity provider | D. Service provider
column6: In this scenario, Active Directory authenticates users via SSO for the employee portal. Active Directory holds user credentials and validates them. Although LDAP is technically the 'identity store' (A), in the context of SSO, Active Directory acts as the Identity Provider (C) — it authenticates users and (via ADFS or another federation service) generates assertions for the portal.

column3: A financial enterprise isplanning to set up a user authentication mechanism to login to the Salesforce system.Due to regulatory requirements, the CIO of the company wants user administration, including passwords and authentication requests, to be managed by an external system that is only accessible via a SOAP webservice.Which authentication mechanism should an identity architect recommend to meet the requirements?
column4: A. OAuth Web-Server Flow | B. Identity Connect | C. Delegated Authentication | D. Just-in-Time Provisioning
column6: Delegated Authentication allows Salesforce to delegate credential validation to an external SOAP web service. The external system (mainframe/SOAP service) receives the username and password from Salesforce and validates them. This satisfies the requirement of having an external SOAP-based authentication system handle all password and authentication requests. JIT provisioning (D) handles user creation, not authentication.

column3: An identity architect has built a native mobile application and plans to integrate it with a Salesforce Identity solution. The following are the requirements for the solution:1. Users should not have to login every time they use the app.2. The app should be able to make calls to the Salesforce REST API.3. End users should NOT see the OAuth approval page.How should the identity architect configure the Salesforce connected app to meet the requirements?
column4: A. Enable the API Scope and Offline Access Scope, upload a certificate so JWT Bearer Flow can be used and then set the connected app access settings to "Admin Pre-Approved". | B. Enable the API Scope and Offline Access Scope on the connected app, and then set the connected app to access settings to 'Admin Pre-Approved". | C. Enable the Full Access Scope and then set the connected app access settings to "Admin Pre-Approved". | D. Enable the API Scope and Offline Access Scope on the connected app, and then set the Connected App access settings to "User may self authorize".
column6: To meet all requirements (no re-login, REST API access, no OAuth approval page): (A) Upload a certificate (for JWT Bearer Flow) so users don't need to approve; enable API and Offline Access scopes (for refresh tokens and API access); set 'Admin Pre-Approved' so users don't see the OAuth approval page. Using refresh tokens (B) alone doesn't prevent the approval page, and Full Access scope (C) is unnecessarily broad.

column3: The security team at Universal Containers (UC) has identified exporting reports as a high-riskaction and would like to require users to be logged into Salesforce with their Active Directory (AD) credentials when doing so. For all other users of Salesforce, users should be allowed to use AD Credentials or Salesforce credentials. What solution should be recommended to prevent exporting reports except when logged in using AD credentials while maintaining the ability to view reports when logged in with Salesforce credentials?
column4: A. Use SAML Federated Authentication and block access to reports when accessed through a Standard Assurance session. | B. Use SAML Federated Authentication and Custom SAML JIT Provisioning to dynamically and or remove a permission set that grants the Export Reports Permission. | C. Use SAML federated Authentication, treat SAML Sessionsas High Assurance, and raise the session level required for exporting reports. | D. Use SAML federated Authentication with a Login Flow to dynamically add or remove a Permission Set that grants the Export Reports Permission.
column6: By configuring SAML sessions as High Assurance and raising the session security level required for the 'Export Reports' operation (in Session Security Level Settings), only users who authenticated via SAML (AD credentials at High Assurance level) can export reports. Users logged in with standard Salesforce credentials (Standard Assurance) can still view reports but cannot export them.

column3: Users logging into Salesforce are frequently prompted to verify their identity.The identity architect is required to provide recommendations so that frequency of prompt verification can be reduced.What should the identity architect recommend to meet the requirement?
column4: A. Implement 2FA authentication for the Salesforce org. | B. Set trusted IP ranges for the organization. | C. Implement a single sign-on for Salesforce using an externalidentity provider. | D. Implement multi-factor authentication for the Salesforce org.
column6: Frequent identity verification prompts typically occur when Salesforce cannot recognize the user's device or location as trusted. Setting Trusted IP Ranges at the organization level (or profile level) tells Salesforce to trust logins from corporate network IPs, eliminating the verification challenge for users logging in from known locations. This reduces verification frequency without disabling security.

column3: Universal containers (UC) wants users to authenticate into their salesforceorg using credentials stored in a custom identity store. UC does not want to purchase or use a third-party Identity provider. Additionally, UC is extremely wary of social media and does not consider it to be trust worthy. Which two options should an architect recommend to UC? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Use a professional social media such as LinkedIn as an Authentication provider | B. Build a custom web page that uses the identity store and calls frontdoor.jsp | C. Build a custom Web service that is supported byDelegated Authentication. | D. Implement the Openid protocol and configure an authentication provider
column6: To authenticate using a custom identity store without a third-party IdP and without social media: (C) Build a custom SOAP/REST web service backed by the identity store and configure Delegated Authentication — Salesforce passes credentials to the web service for validation; (D) Implement the OpenID Connect protocol with the custom identity store as an OIDC provider and configure an Authentication Provider. Both are non-third-party, non-social approaches.

column3: A leadingfitness tracker company is getting ready to launch a customer community. The company wants its customers to login to the community and connect their fitness device to their profile. Customers should be able to obtain exercise details and fitness recommendation in the community.Which should be used to satisfy this requirement?
column4: A. Named Credentials | B. Login Flows | C. OAuth Device Flow | D. Single Sign-On Settings
column6: OAuth 2.0 Device Flow is designed for devices with limited input/output capabilities (like fitness trackers) that need to connect to a user's profile. The device displays a code, the user authenticates on a separate device (phone/browser), and the fitness tracker obtains an access token. This enables customers to connect their fitness devices to their community profile.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) wants to implement SAML SSO for their internal of Salesforce users using a third- party IdP. After some evaluation, UC decides NOT to 65« set up My Domain fortheir Salesforce org. How does that decision impact their SSO implementation?
column4: A. IdP-initiated SSO will NOT work. | B. Neither SP- nor IdP-initiated SSO will work. | C. Either SP- or IdP-initiated SSO will work. | D. SP-initiated SSO will NOT work
column6: My Domain is required for SP-initiated SSO in Salesforce. Without My Domain, Salesforce cannot initiate the redirect to the external IdP because there is no org-specific URL to redirect from. IdP-initiated SSO (C) can still work without My Domain because the IdP redirects to a specific ACS URL. Therefore, SP-initiated SSO will NOT work without My Domain.

column3: A global company is using the Salesforce Platform as an Identity Provider and needs to integrate a third-party application with its Experience Cloud customer portal.Which two features should be utilized to provide users with loginand identity services for the third-party application?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Use the App Launcher with single sign-on (SSO). | B. External a Data source with Named Principal identity type. | C. Use a connected app. | D. Use Delegated Authentication.
column6: When Salesforce Platform acts as an IdP for a third-party application integrated with Experience Cloud: (A) App Launcher with SSO allows users to click a tile in the Salesforce App Launcher to access the third-party app via SSO (IdP-initiated); (C) A Connected App represents the third-party application in Salesforce and enables SAML/OAuth-based identity federation. Named Credentials (B) are for outbound API calls, not IdP functionality.

column3: A group of users try to access one of Universal Containers' Connected Apps and receive the following error message: " Failed: Not approved for access." What is the most likely cause of this issue?
column4: A. The Connected App settings "All users mayself-authorize" is enabled. | B. The Salesforce Administrators have revoked the OAuth authorization. | C. The Users do not have the correct permission set assigned to them. | D. The User of High Assurance sessions are required for the Connected App.
column6: The 'Failed: Not approved for access' error means the connected app's OAuth policy is set to require admin-approved access, and the user does not have the correct permission set assigned that grants access to this connected app. The administrator must assign the appropriate permission set to the user.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) uses Salesforce Experience Cloud sites (previously known as Customer Community) to provide a digital portal where customers can login using theirGoogle account.NTO would like to automatically create a case record for first time users logging into Salesforce Experience Cloud.What should an Identity architect do to fulfill the requirement?
column4: A. Configure an authentication provider for Social Login using Google and a custom registration handler. | B. Implement a Just-in-Time handler class that has logic to create cases upon first login. | C. Create an authentication provider for Social Login using Google and leverage standard registration handler. | D. Implement a login flow with a record create component for Case.
column6: To automatically create a Case record the first time a user logs in via Google authentication, the best approach is to implement a Login Flow with a record-create component. The login flow fires after every login and can check if the user is logging in for the first time (using a custom field), then create a Case record. The registration handler handles user creation, not business record creation. [CORRECTED from A to D — login flow handles post-registration business logic]

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters would like to use a portal built on Salesforce Experience Cloud for customer self- service. Guests of the portal be able toself-register, but be unable to automatically be assigned to a contact record until verified. External Identity licenses have been purchased for the project.After registered guests complete an onboarding process, a flow will create the appropriate accountand contact records for the user.Which three steps should an identity architect follow to implement the outlined requirements?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Enable "Allow customers and partners to self-register". | B. Select the "Configurable Self-Reg Page" optionunder Login & Registration. | C. Set jp an external login page and call Salesforce APIs for user creation. | D. Customize the self-registration Apex handler to temporarily associate the user to a shared single contact record. | E. Customize me self-registrationApex handler to create only the user record.
column6: For External Identity users who self-register but need verification before being linked to a Contact: (A) Enable 'Allow customers and partners to self-register' to enable self-registration; (B) Select 'Configurable Self-Reg Page' to use a custom registration flow; (E) Customize the self-registration Apex handler to create only the user record (without a Contact) initially — the contact and account are created later by the onboarding flow after verification.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has Active Directory (AD) as their enterprise identity store and wouldlike to use it for Salesforce user authentication. UC expects to synchronize user data between Salesforce and AD and Assign the appropriate Profile and Permission Sets based on AD group membership. What would be the optimal way to implement SSO?
column4: A. Use Active Directory with Reverse Proxy as the Identity Provider. | B. Use Microsoft Access control Service as the Authentication provider. | C. Use Active Directory Federation Service (ADFS) as the Identity Provider. | D. Use Salesforce Identity Connect as the Identity Provider.
column6: Salesforce Identity Connect provides AD-to-Salesforce user synchronization and can act as an IdP using SAML SSO. It supports syncing profiles, roles, and permission sets based on AD group membership. While ADFS (C) can act as an IdP, it doesn't sync user attributes to Salesforce. Identity Connect provides both SSO and bidirectional user data sync, making it the optimal choice.

column3: Universal Containers is creating a web application that will be secured by SalesforceIdentity using the OAuth2.0 Web Server Flow uses the OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant type).Which three OAuth concepts apply to this flow?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Verification URL | B. Client Secret | C. Access Token | D. Scopes
column6: Web Server Flow (OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant) involves: (B) Client Secret — the server uses the client secret to exchange the authorization code for tokens; (C) Access Token — the final output is an access token for API calls; (D) Scopes — define what permissions the app is requesting. Verification URL (A) is not part of the standard OAuth Web Server Flow (it's part of the Device Flow).

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has a strict requirement to authenticate users to Salesforce using their mainframe credentials. The mainframe user store cannot be accessed from aSAML provider. UC would also like to have users in Salesforce created on the fly if they provide accurate mainframecredentials.How can the Architect meet these requirements?
column4: A. Use a Salesforce Login Flow to call out to a web service and create the user on the fly. | B. Use the SOAP API to create the user when created on the mainframe; implement Delegated Authentication. | C. Implement Just-In-Time Provisioning on the mainframe to create the user on the fly. | D. Implement OAuth User-Agent Flow on the mainframe; use a Registration Handler to create the user on the fly.
column6: The question states the mainframe user store 'cannot be accessed from a SAML provider', so standard SAML-based JIT provisioning won't work. The correct solution is to implement Delegated Authentication (the mainframe validates credentials via SOAP) and use the SOAP API to pre-create users in Salesforce, OR use a Login Flow to callout to the mainframe and create the user on the fly. Answer C (JIT on mainframe) is incorrect since SAML cannot access the mainframe. Answer A (Login Flow with callout) is the correct approach for on-the-fly user creation. [CORRECTED from C to A]

column3: Which three types of attacks would a 2-Factor Authentication solution help garden against?
column4: A. Key logging attacks | B. Network perimeter attacks | C. Phishing attacks | D. Dictionary attacks | E. Man-in-the-middle attacks
column6: 2FA protects against: (A) Keylogging — even if keystrokes capture the password, the second factor is required; (C) Phishing — even if credentials are stolen via phishing, the second factor blocks access; (D) Dictionary attacks — even if the password is guessed or cracked, the second factor prevents access. MFA does NOT fully protect against man-in-the-middle attacks (E) in real-time (attacker can relay the second factor). Network perimeter attacks (B) are mitigated by network security, not MFA. The question says 'three types' but 4 answers are listed (A,C,D,E) — the correct set of 3 would be A,C,D. [CORRECTED from A,C,D,E to A,C,D]

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) wants to improve its engagement with existing customers to boost customer loyalty. To get a better understanding of its customers, NTO establishes a single customer view including their buying behaviors, channel preferences and purchasing history. All of this information exists but is spread across different systems and formats.NTO has decided to use Salesforce as the platform to build a 360 degree view. The company already uses Microsoft Active Directory (AD) to manage its users and company assets.What should an Identity Architect do to provision, deprovision and authenticate users?
column4: A. Salesforce Identity is not needed since NTO uses Microsoft AD. | B. Salesforce Identity can be included but NTO will be required to build a custom integration with Microsoft AD. | C. Salesforce Identity is included in the Salesforce licenses so it does not need to be considered separately. | D. A Salesforce Identity can be included but NTO will require Identity Connect.
column6: Salesforce Identity Connect integrates Microsoft Active Directory with Salesforce, providing: automated user provisioning and deprovisioning based on AD changes, SAML-based SSO, and profile/permission assignment based on AD groups. It is the optimal solution for organizations that already use Microsoft AD and want to manage Salesforce users from AD.

column3: Which two statements are capable of Identity Connect? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Synchronization of Salesforce Permission Set Licence Assignments. | B. Supports both Identity-Provider-Initiated and Service-Provider-Initiated SSO. | C. Support multiple orgs connecting to multiple Active Directory servers. | D. Automated user synchronization and de-activation.
column6: Identity Connect capabilities: (B) Supports both IdP-initiated and SP-initiated SSO — it can handle SAML flows in both directions; (D) Automated user synchronization and de-activation — it syncs user data and status changes from AD to Salesforce automatically. Identity Connect does NOT sync Permission Set License Assignments (A) and does NOT support multiple orgs with multiple AD servers (C) out of the box.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) uses Salesforce for Sales Opportunity Management. Okta was recently brought in to Just-in-Time (JIT) provision and authenticate NTO users to applications. Salesforce users also use Oktato authorize a Forecasting web application to access Salesforce records on their behalf.Which two roles are being performed by Salesforce?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. SAML Identity Provider | B. OAuth Client | C. OAuth Resource Server | D. SAML Service Provider
column6: In this scenario: Okta authenticates NTO users and uses JIT to provision them (Okta is IdP, Salesforce is SAML SP). Salesforce users also use Okta to authorize a Forecasting web app to access Salesforce records (Salesforce acts as OAuth Resource Server, and the web app is the OAuth Client). Wait — the question says 'Salesforce users use Okta to authorize a Forecasting web application to access Salesforce records on their behalf.' This means Salesforce is the OAuth Resource Server (C) and NTO users act through Okta (Salesforce is SAML SP for JIT = D). Therefore B,D is correct: Salesforce is OAuth Client (incorrect) vs Salesforce is SAML SP (D) and OAuth Resource Server (C). Re-reading: the Forecasting app uses Okta authorization to access Salesforce — so Salesforce is the resource server. And for SAML JIT from Okta — Salesforce is the SAML SP. Correct answer should be C,D. [CORRECTED from B,D to C,D]

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) uses the Customer 360 Platform implemented on Salesforce Experience Cloud. The development team in charge has learned of a contactless user feature, which can reduce the overhead of managing customers and partners by creating users without contact information.What is the potential impact to the architecture if NTO decides to implement this feature?
column4: A. Custom registration handler is needed to correctly assign External Identity or Community license for the newly registered contactless user. | B. If contactless user is upgraded to Community license, the contact record is automatically created and linked to the user record, but not associated with an Account. | C. Contactless user feature is available only with the External Identity license, which can restrict the ExperienceCloud functionality available to the user. | D. Passwordless authentication cannot be supported because the mobile phone receiving one-time password (OTP) needs to match the number on the contact record.
column6: Contactless user feature: When a contactless user is upgraded from External Identity license to Community (Customer Community) license, Salesforce automatically creates and links a Contact record but does NOT associate it with an Account record. This means the user's contact exists without an account association, which may impact data sharing and community access. This is the key architectural impact to understand.

column3: An organization has a central cloud-based Identity and Access Management (IAM) Service for authentication and user management, which must be utilized by all applications as follows:1 - Change of a user status in the central IAM Service triggers provisioning or deprovisioning in the integrated cloud applications.2 - Security Assertion Markup Language single sign-on (SSO) is used to facilitate access for users authenticated atidentity provider (Central IAM Service).Which approach should an IAM architect implement on Salesforce Sales Cloud to meet the requirements?
column4: A. A Configure Salesforce as a SAML Service Provider, and enable SCIM (System for Cross-Domain Identity Management) for provisioning and deprovisioning of users. | B. Configure Salesforce as a SAML service provider, and enable Just-in Time (JIT) provisioning and deprovisioning of users. | C. Configure central IAM Service as an authentication provider and extend registration handler to manage provisioning and deprovisioning of users. | D. Deploy Identity Connect component and set up automated provisioning and deprovisioning of users, as well as SAML-based SSO.
column6: The requirement has two parts: (1) SAML SSO with central IAM as IdP (Salesforce is SP), and (2) SCIM-based provisioning/deprovisioning triggered by the central IAM. Salesforce supports SCIM 2.0 as both a provider and consumer, allowing the central IAM to push user changes to Salesforce via SCIM API. This is the industry-standard approach for automated identity lifecycle management.

column3: A technology enterprise is setting up an identity solution with an external vendors wellness application for its employees. The user attributes need to be returned to the wellness application in an ID token.Which authentication mechanism should an identity architect recommend to meet therequirements?
column4: A. OpenID Connect | B. User Agent Flow | C. JWT Bearer Token Flow | D. Web Server Flow
column6: OpenID Connect (OIDC) extends OAuth 2.0 with an ID token (JWT) that contains user attribute claims. It is specifically designed to return user profile attributes to the relying party (the wellness application) via the ID token. OAuth 2.0 flows (B, C, D) alone provide access tokens but not ID tokens with user attributes.

column3: What is oneof the roles of an Identity Provider in a Single Sign-on setup using SAML?
column4: A. Validate token | B. Create token | C. Consume token | D. Revoke token
column6: In SAML SSO, the Identity Provider (IdP) is responsible for creating (generating) the SAML token (assertion) after authenticating the user. The Service Provider consumes the token. The IdP does not validate, consume, or revoke tokens — it creates them and sends them to the SP.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) has an existing custom business-to-consumer (B2C) website that does NOT support single sign-on standards, such as Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) or OAuth. NTO wants to use Salesforce Identity to register and authenticate new customers on the website.Which two Salesforce features should an identity architect use in order to provide username/password authentication for the website?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Identity Connect | B. Delegated Authentication | C. Connected Apps | D. Embedded Login
column6: For a B2C website that does not support SSO standards (SAML/OAuth): (B) Delegated Authentication allows the website to call Salesforce's authentication service via SOAP, delegating credential validation to Salesforce Identity; (D) Embedded Login provides a JavaScript-based login widget that can be embedded in any website, allowing customers to authenticate against Salesforce without SAML/OAuth. Identity Connect (A) is for AD sync, not B2C.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is using a custom application that will act as the Identity Provider and will generate SAML assertions used to log in to Salesforce. UC is considering including custom parameters in the SAML assertion. These attributes contain sensitive data and are needed to authenticate the users. The assertions are submitted to salesforce via a browser form post. The majority of the users will only be able to access Salesforce via UC's corporate network, but a subset of admins and executives would be allowed access from outside the corporate network on their mobile devices. Which two methods should an Architect consider to ensure that the sensitive data cannot be tampered with, nor accessible to anyone while in transit?
column4: A. Use the Identity Provider's certificate to digitally sign and Salesforce's Certificate to encryptthe payload. | B. Use Salesforce's Certificate to digitally sign the SAML Assertion and a Mobile Device Management client on the users' mobile devices. | C. Use the Identity provider's certificate to digitally Sign and the Identity provider's certificate toencrypt the payload. | D. Use a custom login flow to retrieve sensitive data using an Apex callout without including the attributes in the assertion.
column6: To protect sensitive SAML assertion data from tampering and unauthorized access while in transit: (C) Use the Identity Provider's certificate to digitally sign the assertion (proving authenticity and preventing tampering) and the Identity Provider's certificate to encrypt the payload (protecting sensitive data from interception); (D) Use a custom login flow with Apex to retrieve sensitive data at the Salesforce side via callout, avoiding including it in the assertion entirely.

column3: Universal Containers is using OpenID Connect to enable a connection from their new mobile app to its production Salesforce org.What should be done to enable the retrieval of the access token status for the OpenID Connect connection?
column4: A. Query using OpenIDConnect discovery endpoint. | B. A Leverage OpenID Connect Token Introspection. | C. Create a custom OAuth scope. | D. Enable cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) for the /services/oauth2/token endpoint.
column6: OpenID Connect Token Introspection allows an application to query the authorization server about the current state (validity, expiry, scopes) of an access token. This is the correct mechanism to retrieve and check the status of an OpenID Connect access token in Salesforce without making a full user-info request.

column3: An insurance company has a connected app in its Salesforce environment that is usedto integrate with a Google Workspace (formerly known as G Suite).An identity and access management (IAM) architect has been asked to implement automation to enable users, freeze/suspend users, disable users, and reactivate existing users in Google Workspace upon similar actions in Salesforce.Which solution is recommended to meet this requirement?
column4: A. Configure user Provisioning for Connected Apps. | B. Update the Security Assertion Markup Language Just-in-Time (SAML JIT) handler in Salesforce for user provisioning and de-provisioning. | C. Build a custom REST endpoint in Salesforce that Google Workspace can poll against. | D. Build an Apex trigger on the userlogin object to make asynchronous callouts to Google APIs.
column6: User Provisioning for Connected Apps in Salesforce allows administrators to configure automated user lifecycle management (create, freeze, disable, reactivate) for connected external applications like Google Workspace. When a user's status changes in Salesforce, the provisioning framework automatically sends the corresponding API calls to the connected app (Google Workspace).

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) wants to give customers the ability to submit and manage issues with their purchases. It is important for NTO to give its customers the ability to login with their Amazon credentials.What should an identity architect recommend to meet these requirements?
column4: A. Configure a predefined authentication provider for Amazon. | B. Create a custom external authentication provider for Amazon. | C. Configure an OpenID Connect Authentication Provider for Amazon. | D. Configure Amazon as a connected app.
column6: Amazon does not have a predefined Salesforce authentication provider (unlike Facebook, Twitter, Google). However, Amazon supports OpenID Connect (OIDC). Therefore, the architect should configure an OpenID Connect Authentication Provider in Salesforce and use Amazon's OIDC endpoints. Building a fully custom provider (B) is unnecessary when OIDC works.

column3: customer service representatives at Universal containers (UC) are complaining that whenever they click on links to case records and are asked to login with SAML SSO, they are beingredirected to the salesforce home tab and not the specific case record. What item should an architect advise the identity team at UC to investigate first?
column4: A. My domain is configured and active within salesforce. | B. The salesforce SSO settings are using http post | C. The identity provider is correctly preserving the Relay state | D. The users have the correct Federation ID within salesforce.
column6: When users are redirected to the Salesforce home page instead of the specific case record after SAML SSO, the issue is that the Identity Provider is not correctly preserving or passing the RelayState parameter. The RelayState carries the deep link URL (the specific case record URL) back to Salesforce after authentication. Without it, Salesforce defaults to the home page.

column3: Universal containers (UC) is building a mobile application that will make calls to the salesforce RESTAPI.Additionally, UC would like to provide the optimal experience for its mobile users. Which two OAuth scopes should UC configure in the connected App? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Refresh token | B. API | C. full | D. Web
column6: For a mobile app making Salesforce REST API calls with optimal user experience: (A) Refresh Token scope enables the app to obtain new access tokens without requiring the user to re-authenticate, providing seamless background token refresh; (B) API scope grants access to the Salesforce REST API. Full access (C) is unnecessarily broad; Web scope (D) is for web-based OAuth flows, not mobile REST API calls.

column3: Universal Containers wants to secure its Salesforce APIs by using an existing Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) configuration supports the company's single sign-on process to Salesforce, Which Salesforce OAuth authorization flow should be used?
column4: A. OAuth 2.0 SAML Bearer AssertionFlow | B. A SAML Assertion Flow | C. OAuth 2.0 User-Agent Flow | D. OAuth 2.0 JWT Bearer Flow
column6: OAuth 2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion Flow allows a client to exchange an existing SAML assertion (from the SSO flow) for a Salesforce OAuth access token. This leverages the existing SAML SSO configuration to secure API access without requiring additional authentication, enabling server-to-server API calls using the SAML trust already established.

column3: Universal containers want to build a custom mobile app connecting to salesforce using Oauth, and would like to restrict the types of resources mobile users can access. What Oauth feature of Salesforce should be used to achieve the goal?
column4: A. Access Tokens | B. Mobile pins | C. Refresh Tokens | D. Scopes
column6: OAuth Scopes define what resources and operations a connected app can access on behalf of a user. By configuring specific scopes (e.g., api, web, chatter_api, custom_permissions), the administrator restricts what Salesforce resources mobile users can access through the connected app, without changing the user's overall Salesforce permissions.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) is both a Salesforce and Google Apps customer. The UC IT team would like to manage the users for both systems in a single place to reduce administrative burden. Which two optimal ways can the IT team provision users and allow Single Sign-on between Salesforce and Google Apps ? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Build a custom app running on Heroku as the Identity Provider that can sync user information between Salesforce and Google Apps. | B. Use a third-party product as the Identity Provider for both Salesforce and Google Apps and manage the provisioning from there. | C. Use Identity Connect as the Identity Provider for both Salesforce and Google Apps and manage the provisioning from there. | D. UseSalesforce as the Identity Provider and Google Apps as a Service Provider and configure User Provisioning for Connected Apps.
column6: To manage users in a single place and provide SSO between Salesforce and Google Apps: (B) Use a third-party IdP (like Okta or Azure AD) as the central identity provider for both systems, with provisioning managed from the IdP; (D) Use Salesforce as the IdP for Google Apps and configure User Provisioning for Connected Apps to sync users to Google. Identity Connect (C) only manages Salesforce, not Google Apps.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) uses Salesforce to allow customers to keep track of the order status. The customers can log in to Salesforce using external authentication providers, such as Facebook and Google. UC is also leveraging the App Launcher to let customers access an off-platform application for generating shipping labels.The labelgenerator application uses OAuth to provide users access. What license type should an Architect recommend for the customers?
column4: A. Customer Community license | B. Identity license | C. Customer Community Plus license | D. External Identity license
column6: External Identity license is designed for B2C customers who need to log in via social/external identity providers and access non-CRM Salesforce features. It includes App Launcher access and supports OAuth-based access to off-platform applications. Customer Community license (A) doesn't include App Launcher; Identity license (B) is for internal employee SSO.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) wants to integrate a third-party Reward Calculation system with Salesforce to calculate Rewards. Rewards will be calculated on a schedule basis and update back into Salesforce. The integration between Salesforce and the Reward Calculation System needs to be secure. Which are two recommended practices for using OAuth flow in this scenario. choose 2 answers
column4: A. OAuth Refresh Token FLow | B. OAuth Username-Password Flow | C. OAuth SAML Bearer Assertion FLow | D. OAuth JWT Bearer Token FLow
column6: For scheduled server-to-server integration between Salesforce and a reward calculation system: (C) OAuth SAML Bearer Assertion Flow — if SAML SSO is already configured, this exchanges the SAML assertion for an OAuth token for API access; (D) OAuth JWT Bearer Token Flow — uses certificate-based authentication for secure server-to-server calls without stored credentials. Both are suitable for automated, scheduled integrations without user interaction.

column3: Containers (UC) has decided to implement a federated single Sign-on solution using a third-party Idp.In reviewing the third-party products, they would like to ensure the product supports the automated provisioning and deprovisioning of users. What are the underlying mechanisms that the UC Architect must ensure are part of the product?
column4: A. SOAP API for provisioning; Just-in-Time (JIT) for Deprovisioning. | B. Just-In-time (JIT) for Provisioning; SOAP API for Deprovisioning. | C. Provisioning API for both Provisioning and Deprovisioning. | D. Just-in-Time (JIT) for both Provisioning and Deprovisioning.
column6: For automated provisioning and deprovisioning in a federated SSO solution, JIT (Just-in-Time) provisioning handles user creation on first login, and the same SAML-JIT mechanism can handle deprovisioning when specific attributes are set (e.g., setting isActive=false in the SAML assertion deactivates the user). Both provisioning and deprovisioning can be handled via JIT SAML assertions.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has built a custom time tracking app for its employee. UC wants to leverage Salesforce Identity to control access to the custom app.At a minimum, which Salesforce license is required to support this requirement?
column4: A. Identity Verification | B. Identity Connect | C. Identity Only | D. External Identity
column6: Identity Only license is the minimum license required to use Salesforce Identity features (SSO, authentication, app launcher) for employees accessing non-Salesforce applications. It allows users to authenticate to Salesforce and use it as an IdP for other apps without needing full CRM access. External Identity (D) is for external/consumer users.

column3: A technology enterprise is planning to implement single sign-on login for users. When users log in to the Salesforce User object custom field, data should be populated fornew and existing users.Which two steps should an identity architect recommend?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Implement Auth.SamlJitHandler Interface. | B. Create and update methods. | C. Implement RegistrationHandler Interface. | D. Implement SessionManagement Class.
column6: For SAML JIT provisioning with custom Salesforce User object fields: (A) Implement the Auth.SamlJitHandler interface — this interface provides methods for handling JIT user creation and updates with access to SAML assertion attributes; (B) Implement create and update methods in the JIT handler — these methods are called when creating new users or updating existing users during JIT provisioning, allowing custom field population from SAML attributes.

column3: Universal containers(UC) has decided to build a new, highly sensitive application on Force.com platform. The security team at UC has decided that they want users toprovide a fingerprint in addition to username/Password to authenticate to this application.How can an architect support fingerprint as a form of identification for salesforce Authentication?
column4: A. Use salesforce Two-factor Authentication with callouts to a third-party fingerprint scanning application. | B. Use Delegated Authentication with callouts to a third-party fingerprint scanning application. | C. Use an AppExchange product that does fingerprint scanning with native salesforce identity confirmation. | D. Use custom login flows with callouts to a third-party fingerprint scanning application.
column6: Custom Login Flows in Salesforce allow inserting custom authentication steps into the login process. By implementing a Login Flow that makes a callout to a third-party fingerprint scanning service, the architect can integrate biometric authentication as an additional step after the username/password. The flow can enforce this requirement before granting access to the sensitive application.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) currently uses Salesforce Sales Cloud and an external billing application. Both Salesforce andthe billing application are accessed several times a day to manage customers. UC would like to configure single sign-on and leverageSalesforce as the identity provider. Additionally, UC would like the billing application to be accessible from Salesforce.A redirect is acceptable.Which two Salesforce tools should an identity architect recommend to satisfy the requirements?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. salesforce Canvas | B. Identity Connect | C. Connected Apps | D. App Launcher
column6: To configure SSO with Salesforce as IdP and make the billing application accessible from Salesforce: (A) Salesforce Canvas displays the external billing application within Salesforce, providing seamless access with a redirect; (D) App Launcher allows users to click a tile in Salesforce to launch the billing application via SSO (IdP-initiated). Identity Connect (B) is for AD sync; Connected Apps (C) is needed but not the interface tool.

column3: Universal containers (UC) wants to implement Delegated Authentication for a certainsubset of Salesforce users. Which three items should UC take into consideration while building the Web service to handle the Delegated Authentication request? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. The web service needs to include Source IP as a method parameter. | B. UC should whitelist all salesforce ip ranges on their corporate firewall. | C. The web service can be written using either the soap or rest protocol. | D. Delegated Authentication is enabled for the system administrator profile. | E. The return type of the Web service method should be a Boolean value
column6: When building the Delegated Authentication web service: (A) Include Source IP as a method parameter — Salesforce passes the client IP to the web service, allowing IP-based access control; (B) Whitelist Salesforce IP ranges on the corporate firewall — the web service endpoint must accept SOAP calls from Salesforce's IP ranges; (E) The return type must be Boolean — the web service returns true (authenticated) or false (rejected). The protocol can only be SOAP (C is wrong — must be SOAP, not REST).

column3: Universal containers wants to implement single Sign-on for a salesforce org using an external identity provider and corporate identity store. What type of Authentication flow is required to support deep linking?
column4: A. Web server Oauth SSO flow. | B. Identity-provider-initiated SSO | C. Service-provider-initiated SSO | D. Start URL on identity provider
column6: Deep linking in SSO requires the user's intended destination URL to be preserved and restored after authentication. SP-initiated SSO uses the RelayState parameter to carry the deep link URL to the IdP and back. With SP-initiated SSO, the user starts at the Salesforce URL (e.g., a specific record), Salesforce sends them to the IdP with the RelayState containing the return URL, and after authentication, the user is redirected to the original page.

column3: architect is troubleshooting some SAML-based SSO errors during testing. The Architect confirmed that all of the Salesforce SSO settings are correct. Which two issues outside of the Salesforce SSO settings are most likely contributing to the SSO errors the Architect is encountering? Choose 2 Answers
column4: A. The Identity Provider is also used to SSO into five other applications. | B. The clock on the Identity Provider server is twenty minutes behind Salesforce. | C. The Issuer Certificate from the Identity Provider expired two weeks ago. | D. The default language for the Identity Provider and Salesforce are Different.
column6: SAML SSO errors outside Salesforce settings: (B) IdP clock 20 minutes behind Salesforce — SAML assertions have NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter timestamps; Salesforce rejects assertions with timestamps more than 5-8 minutes out of sync; (C) IdP certificate expired 2 weeks ago — Salesforce validates the assertion signature against the uploaded certificate; an expired certificate causes signature validation failure. Language differences (D) and multiple applications using the same IdP (A) don't cause SAML errors.

column3: A global fitness equipment manufacturer is planning to sell fitness tracking devices and has the following requirements:1) Customer purchases the device.2) Customer registers the device using their mobile app.3) A case should automatically be createdin Salesforce and associated with the customer's account in cases where the device registers issues with tracking.Which OAuth flow should be used to meet these requirements?
column4: A. OAuth 2.0 Asset Token Flow | B. OAuth 2.0 Username-Password Flow | C. OAuth 2.0User-Agent Flow | D. OAuth 2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion Flow
column6: OAuth 2.0 Asset Token Flow is designed for IoT and connected devices that are registered as digital assets. In this scenario, the fitness tracking device is registered by the customer, and when the device detects tracking issues, it needs to create a Salesforce Case. The Asset Token Flow authenticates the device (not a user) and enables it to make API calls to Salesforce to create the case.

column3: Which two are valid choices for digital certificates when setting up two-way SSL between Salesforce and an external system. Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Use a trusted CA-signed certificate for salesforce and a trusted CA-signed cert for the external system | B. Use a trusted CA-signed certificate for salesforce and a self-signed cert for the external system | C. Use a self-signed certificate for salesforce and a self-signed cert for the external system | D. Use a self-signed certificate for salesforce and a trusted CA-signed cert for the external system
column6: For two-way SSL between Salesforce and an external system: (C) Self-signed certificate for Salesforce + self-signed certificate for the external system — both sides can present and accept self-signed certs when they are explicitly trusted/uploaded to each side; (D) Self-signed certificate for Salesforce + CA-signed cert for the external system — Salesforce's self-signed cert is uploaded to the external system; the CA-signed cert is automatically trusted. CA-signed certs (A, B) for Salesforce are valid but the question asks for 'two valid choices' including self-signed options.

column3: Universal containers wants to set up SSO for a selected group of users to access external applications from salesforce through App launcher. Which three steps must be completed in salesforceto accomplish the goal?
column4: A. Associate user profiles with the connected Apps. | B. Complete my domain and Identity provider setup. | C. Create connected apps for the external applications. | D. Complete single Sign-on settings in security controls. | E. Create named credentials for each external system.
column6: To set up SSO for selected users accessing external apps via App Launcher: (A) Associate user profiles (or permission sets) with the connected apps to control which users have access; (B) Complete My Domain setup (for SP-initiated SSO) and Identity Provider setup (to issue SAML assertions to external apps); (C) Create connected apps for each external application with SAML or OAuth configuration. Single Sign-On settings in security controls (D) is part of the setup but optional if IdP is configured separately.

column3: An architect has successfully configuredSAML-BASED SSO for universal containers. SSO has been working for 3 months when Universal containers manually adds a batch of new users to salesforce. The new users receive an error from salesforce when trying to use SSO. Existing users are still able to successfully use SSO to access salesforce. What is the probable cause of this behaviour?
column4: A. The administrator forgot to reset the new user's salesforce password. | B. The Federation ID field on the new user records is not correctly set | C. The my domaincapability is not enabled on the new user's profile. | D. The new users do not have the SSO permission enabled on their profiles.
column6: When new users added manually cannot use SSO while existing users can, the most likely cause is that the Federation ID field was not populated on the new user records. The Federation ID must match the NameID value in the SAML assertion from the IdP. Without it, Salesforce cannot match the assertion to a user. My Domain doesn't need to be enabled per profile (C), and the SSO permission (D) is not typically profile-based.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) uses Global Shipping (GS) as one of their shipping vendors. Regional leads of GS need access to UC's Salesforce instance for reporting damage of goods using Cases. The regional leads also need access to dashboards to keep track ofregional shipping KPIs. UC internally uses a third-party cloud analytics tool forcapacity planning and UC decided to provide access to this tool to a subset of GS employees.In addition to regional leads, the GS capacity planning team would benefit fromaccess to this tool. To access the analytics tool, UC IT has set up Salesforce as the Identity provider for Internal users and would like to follow the same approach for the GS users as well. What are the most appropriate license types for GS Tregional Leads and the GS Capacity Planners? Choose 2 Answers
column4: A. Customer Community Plus license for GS Regional Leads and External Identity for GS Capacity Planners. | B. Customer Community Plus license for GS Regional Leads and Customer Community license for GS Capacity Planners. | C. Identity License for GS Regional Leads and External Identity license for GS capacity Planners. | D. Customer Community license for GS Regional Leads and Identity license for GS Capacity Planners.
column6: GS Regional Leads need Case access and dashboard access — Customer Community Plus license provides Salesforce objects (Cases, Dashboards) access for external users. GS Capacity Planners only need SSO access to the analytics tool (via App Launcher/IdP) without full community access — Identity license provides IdP/SSO functionality. The answer A,D is correct: Customer Community Plus for Regional Leads (A portion) and Identity license for Capacity Planners (D portion). However, answer D in the choices is 'Customer Community license for GS Regional Leads and Identity license for GS Capacity Planners' — so the combination is A (Customer Community Plus for Leads) and D (Identity license for Planners). This is correct.

column3: Containers (UC) has an existing Customer Community. UC wants to expand the self-registration capabilities such that customers receive a different community experience based on the data they provide during the registration process. What is the recommended approach an Architect Should recommend to UC?
column4: A. Create an After Insert Apextrigger on the user object to assign specific custom permissions. | B. Create separate login flows corresponding to the different community user personas. | C. Modify the Community pages to utilize specific fields on the User and Contact records. | D. Modify theexisting Communities registration controller to assign different profiles.
column6: To provide different community experiences based on self-registration data, the recommended approach is to modify the Community pages to display different content based on specific fields on the User and Contact records (set during registration). This leverages Audience-based content targeting in Experience Builder, where different page variations are shown to users with different field values.

column3: Refer to the exhibit.Outfitters (NTO) is using Experience Cloud as an Identity for its application on Heroku. The application on Heroku should be able to handle two brands, Northern Trail Shoes and Northern Trail Shirts.A user should select either of the two brands in Heroku before logging into the community. The app then performs Authorization using OAuth2.0 with the Salesforce Experience Cloud site.NTO wants to make sure it renders login page images dynamically based on the user's brand preference selected in Heroku before Authorization.what should an identity architect do to fulfill the above requirements?
column4: A. For each brand create different communities and redirect users to the appropriate community using a custom Login controller written in Apex. | B. Create multiple login screens using Experience Builder and use Login Flows at runtime to route to different login screens. | C. Authorize third-party service by sending authorization requests to the community-url/services/oauth2/authorize/cookie_value. | D. Authorize third-party service by sending authorization requests to the community-url/services/oauth2/authonze/expid_value.
column6: To render login page images dynamically based on brand preference selected in Heroku before OAuth authorization, the Heroku app should send the authorization request to the Experience Cloud URL with an 'expid_value' parameter (expid = Experience ID). Salesforce uses the Experience ID to determine which brand's login page to display. This is the standard mechanism for dynamic login page branding in Salesforce.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters wants to implement a partner community. Active community users will need to review and accept the community rules, and update key contact information for each community member before their annual partner event.Which approach will meet this requirement?
column4: A. Create tasks for users who need toupdate their data or accept the new community rules. | B. Create a custom landing page and email campaign asking all community members to login and verify their data. | C. Create a login flow that conditionally prompts users who have not accepted the newcommunity rules and who have missing or outdated information. | D. Add a banner to the community Home page asking users to update their profile and accept the new community rules.
column6: A Login Flow is the recommended approach to conditionally prompt users to complete required actions. The login flow can check whether the user has accepted the new community rules and whether their contact information is current. If not, it presents the appropriate screens. This runs at login time, ensuring all active users encounter the prompt without requiring email campaigns or manual outreach.

column3: A financial services company uses Salesforce and has a compliance requirement to track information about devices from whichusers log in. Also, a Salesforce Security Administrator needs to have the ability to revoke the device from which users log in.What should be used to fulfill this requirement?
column4: A. Use multi-factor authentication (MFA) to meet the compliance requirement totrack device information. | B. Use the Activations feature to meet the compliance requirement to track device information. | C. Use the Login History object to track information about devices from which users log in. | D. Use Login Flows to capture device fromwhich users log in and store device and user information in a custom object.
column6: The Activations feature in Salesforce tracks device information (browser fingerprint, device type) for each login. When a user logs in from a new device, they receive a verification challenge. Administrators can view activated devices per user and revoke specific device activations from the user's record, meeting both the compliance tracking requirement and the admin revocation requirement.

column3: Which tool should be used to track login data, such as the average number of logins, who logged in more thanthe average number of times and who logged in during non-business hours?
column4: A. Login Inspector | B. Login History | C. Login Report | D. Login Forensics
column6: Login Forensics is an add-on feature that provides advanced login analytics including: average logins per user, users logging in more than average, logins outside business hours, and anomalous login patterns. It is specifically designed for detecting fraudulent or unusual login activity at scale. Login History (B) provides raw data but not the analytical views Login Forensics offers.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has an existing e-commerce platform and is implementing a new customer community. They do not want to force customers to register on both applications due to concern over the customers experience. It is expected that 25% ofthe e-commerce customers will utilize the customer community . The e-commerce platform is capable of generating SAML responses and has an existing REST- ful API capable of managing users. How should UC create the identities of its e-commerce users with thecustomer community?
column4: A. Use SAML JIT in the Customer Community to create users when a user tries to login to the community from the e-commerce site. | B. Use the e-commerce REST API to create users when a user self-register on the customer community and useSAML to allow SSO. | C. Use a nightly batch ETL job to sync users between the Customer Community and the e-commerce platform and use SAML to allow SSO. | D. Use the standard Salesforce API to create users in the Community When a User is Created in the e- Commerce platform and use SAML to allow SSO.
column6: SAML JIT (Just-in-Time) provisioning in the Customer Community automatically creates Salesforce user records when users from the e-commerce platform attempt to log in via SAML SSO. Only the 25% of e-commerce users who actually use the community get Salesforce records created (on demand), avoiding bulk pre-provisioning of all users. This is efficient and respects user experience.

column3: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) is planning to build a new customer service portal and wants to use passwordless login, allowing customers to login with a one-time passcode sent to them via email or SMS.How should the quantity of required Identity Verification Credits be estimated?
column4: A. Each community comes with 10,000 Identity Verification Credits per month and onlycustomers with more than 10,000 logins a month should estimate additional SMS verifications needed. | B. Identity Verification Credits are consumed with each SMS (text message) sent and should be estimated based on the number of login verification challengesfor SMS verification users. | C. Identity Verification Credits are consumed with each verification sent and should be estimated based on the number of loginsthat will incur a verification challenge. | D. Identity Verification Credits are a direct add-on license based on the number of existing member-based or login-based Community licenses.
column6: Identity Verification Credits are consumed each time an SMS (text message) verification is sent. To estimate the required credits: count the number of users who will use SMS verification and multiply by the expected number of login challenges they will face. Email verification does not consume Identity Verification Credits — only SMS-based verification does.

column3: Sales users at Universal containers use salesforce for Opportunity management. Marketing uses a third-party application called Nestfor Lead nurturing that is accessed using username/password. The VP of sales wants to open up access to nest for all sales uses to provide them access to lead history and would like SSO for better adoption. Salesforce is already setup for SSO and uses Delegated Authentication. Nest can accept username/Password or SAML-based Authentication. IT teams have received multiple password-related issues for nest and have decided to set up SSO access for Nest for Marketing users as well. The CIO does not want to invest in a new IDP solution and is considering using Salesforce for this purpose. Which are appropriate license typechoices for sales and marketing users, giving salesforce is using Delegated Authentication? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Salesforce license for sales users and Identity license for Marketing users | B. Salesforce license for sales users and External Identity license for Marketing users | C. Identity license for sales users and Identity connect license for Marketing users | D. Salesforce license for sales usersand platform license for Marketing users.
column6: Sales users already have Salesforce licenses (full access + Delegated Auth for their SSO). Marketing users need to access Nest via Salesforce as IdP but don't need full Salesforce CRM features. A Salesforce Platform license (D option) gives Marketing users enough access to use Salesforce as an IdP for Nest SSO without needing a full Salesforce license. Answer A (Salesforce for sales) and D (Platform for marketing) is correct.

column3: Universal Containerswants to implement Single Sign-on for a Salesforce org using an external Identity Provider and corporate identity store.What type of authentication flow is required to support deep linking'
column4: A. Web Server OAuth SSO flow | B. Service-Provider-Initiated SSO | C. Identity-Provider-initiated SSO | D. StartURL on Identity Provider
column6: SP-initiated SSO with RelayState supports deep linking. When a user clicks a deep link to a Salesforce resource, Salesforce initiates the SAML flow (SP-initiated), passing the target URL as RelayState. After IdP authentication, the user is redirected to the original deep-linked resource. IdP-initiated SSO (C) doesn't inherently support deep linking from the SP side.

column3: A web service is developed that allows secure access to customer order status on the Salesforce Platform. The service connects to Salesforce through a connected app with the web server flow. The following are the required actions for the authorization flow:1. User Authenticates and Authorizes Access2. Request an Access Token3. Salesforce Grants an Access Token4. Request an Authorization Code5. Salesforce Grants Authorization CodeWhat is the correct sequence for the authorization flow?
column6: Web Server Flow (Authorization Code Grant) sequence: (4) Request Authorization Code → (1) User Authenticates and Authorizes → (5) Salesforce Grants Authorization Code → (2) Request Access Token (using the code) → (3) Salesforce Grants Access Token. The correct sequence is 4→1→5→2→3.

column3: Universal containers (UC) is setting up their customer Community self-registration process. They are uncomfortable with the idea of assigning new users to a default account record. What will happen when customers self-register in the community?
column4: A. The self-registration process will produce an error to the user. | B. The self-registration page will ask user to select an account. | C. The self-registration process will create a person Account record. | D. The self-registration page will create a new account record.
column6: When no default account is configured for self-registration, Salesforce automatically creates a Person Account (if Person Accounts are enabled) for the self-registering user. Person Accounts combine Account and Contact into a single record, which is appropriate for B2C customers without a corporate account association.

column3: How should an identity architect automate provisioning and deprovisioning of users into Salesforce from an external system?
column4: A. Call SOAP API upsert() on user object. | B. Use Security Assertion Markup Language Just-in-Time (SAML JIT) on incoming SAML assertions. | C. Run registration handler on incoming OAuth responses. | D. Call OpenID Connect (OIDC)-userinfo endpoint with a valid access token.
column6: To automate user provisioning from an external system to Salesforce, the correct answer depends on the integration mechanism. SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) is the industry-standard REST API for user provisioning. However, among the given options: (A) SOAP API upsert on the User object is the direct programmatic approach for creating/updating users; (C) registration handler runs on OAuth responses, which is user-initiated; (B) SAML JIT is triggered by SAML assertions, not external system push. The best answer for 'external system pushes user changes to Salesforce' is A (SOAP API upsert). [CORRECTED from C to A]

column3: A company with 15,000 employees is using Salesforce and would like to take the necessary steps to highlight or curb fraudulent activity.Which tool should be used to track login data, such as the average number of logins, who logged in more than the average number of times and who logged in during non-business hours?
column4: A. Login Forensics | B. Login Report | C. Login Inspector | D. Login History
column6: Login Forensics is a Salesforce add-on that provides advanced login analytics: tracking average login frequency, identifying users who log in more than average, detecting logins during non-business hours, and flagging anomalous patterns. It is the tool specifically designed for fraud detection and unusual activity monitoring. Login History (D) provides raw logs but not the analytical insights.
